Everything landed well from the start. The front desk team handled the arrival with a kind of quiet confidence that made the whole check-in feel like running into people who actually wanted you there. The room was tidy and genuinely silent in a way that felt earned rather than accidental, breakfast was simple but done with real attention, and the neighbourhood outside contributed something most hotels can't manufacture: stillness. But the thing that stays with you is the honesty of the value. The level of care here has no business existing at this price point, and that gap between expectation and reality is exactly what makes the place so easy to recommend and so difficult to stay away from.

I visited this hotel briefly for an anime convention, and while I didn’t stay overnight, I had the opportunity to explore its facilities. The back lobby could definitely be larger to accommodate large crowds — during the event, it felt very cramped and difficult to navigate.

The air conditioning was underwhelming, especially considering the number of people in attendance. It got uncomfortably warm, and stronger AC would have made the experience more pleasant. Additionally, the lighting near the elevators, especially on the upper floors, was quite dim and felt unsafe — it could really use improvement.

The in-hotel restaurant was a bit confusing to navigate. The staff seemed inconsistent in enforcing policies about where you could sit or whether outside food was allowed in certain areas. Better communication and flexibility would go a long way.

Overall, the hotel has potential, but these issues — lighting, climate control, and unclear policies — were noticeable during my visit. It may work better for smaller events or guests simply passing through, but for large-scale gatherings, some upgrades are definitely needed.

Overall, my stay was good. The hotel itself was comfortable and my interactions with staff after check-in were genuinely pleasant and professional. Unfortunately, my very first interaction when I arrived left a lasting negative impression.

When I checked in on January 30, I was assisted by a front desk employee whose name tag read Kim. From the start, the interaction felt rushed and dismissive. When I did not immediately understand certain things, she made hand gestures at me to move faster rather than taking a moment to explain. She also didn’t bother to look at me while dismissively gesturing, which made it feel even worse. The overall tone felt impatient and unwelcoming, and I genuinely felt that she did not care how I felt in that moment whatsoever.

Maybe I could have been more prepared coming to the front desk with what she may have needed? Or said something differently? I’m not sure if she thought she could act that at towards me just because I’m younger? I’m just not sure. I was a bit tired after walking through the freezing cold for awhile to get there, but I don’t think any of my actions warranted her response and treatment of me.

I want to be clear that this is not about policy or procedure. It was about how I was treated. As a guest, regardless of age, I expect basic courtesy and respect. That first interaction was my introduction to the hotel, and it set a tone that lingered longer than it should have.

What stood out even more was how different this experience was compared to the rest of your staff, who were consistently kind, helpful, and professional throughout my stay. Because of that contrast, this interaction felt especially disappointing.

I am sharing this feedback with the hope that it helps ensure all guests feel welcomed and respected from the moment they arrive.

We stayed at this hotel and, to be fair, the room was good and very clean. Housekeeping did a great job, and the room itself was comfortable.

However, the “breakfast” is basically a scam. They advertise breakfast, but what you actually get is the option to choose only 4 pre-packaged items from whatever they have available, and you’re not allowed to combine freely or choose anything else. Everything beyond those 4 items is charged extra. Even the bakery products are pre-packaged and they just heat them up on the spot, so it doesn’t feel fresh at all.

If you have the choice, I strongly recommend booking the room without breakfast. There are several great places nearby where you can get a much better breakfast for a fair price.

Since it was summer (and NYC can get hot!), they kindly provided complimentary 500ml bottles of Poland Spring water upon arrival both in 2024 and 2025, which was such a thoughtful gesture to keep guests hydrated.
Overall, this Hilton exceeded my expectations on every visit. Highly recommend it for anyone looking for comfort, convenience, and genuine hospitality in the Big Apple. I'll definitely be back! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

1. 위치 : 맨하탄 35번가 서편에 위치함. 왠만한 주요 랜드마크는 도보로 이동 가능하였음.

- 브라이언트파크,공립도서관 5분
- 록펠러센터 10분
- 타임스퀘어 15분

3. 기타
1) 호텔 바로 옆에 한식, 중식 식당과 까페가 위치해 있어 매우 유용함.
2) 1박 당 50불의 디파짓이 있는데, 체크인 할 때 결제하고 이 금액은 투숙하는 동안 호텔 1층 편의점 이용할 때 디파짓에서 차감되도록 이용할 수도 있음. (물론, 디파짓 차감시키지 않고 따로 결제해도 됨). 해당 디파짓은 체크아웃 시에 페이백 되는 금액임.
3) 별도 리조트 Fee 없음.
4) 4인 가족이 하나의 방에 묵을 수 있는 룸도 있음.
5) 방에서 엠파이어 스테이트 빌딩 조망이 가능함.

We recently stayed at the Hilton Garden Inn on W. 35th St. in Manhattan, and we couldn't be happier with our choice! The location is simply unbeatable. Just a short walk from Penn Station, we found it incredibly easy to navigate the city with public transportation right at our fingertips.

The area is bustling with life, filled with a fantastic array of restaurants, food carts, and shopping options with Macys a stone throw away. Being so close to Madison Square Garden and the iconic Empire State Building was a real treat. In fact, we were lucky enough to have a room with a stunning view of the Empire State Building – it made for some unforgettable moments!

As for the rooms, they are standard for a Hilton Garden Inn, which means they are on the smaller side (as expected in NYC), but they were impeccably clean and well-maintained. The amenities were average, but that didn’t detract from our experience.

One thing to note is that while the hotel doesn't have a full-fledged restaurant, the breakfast option they offered was quite good, albeit a bit different from the typical Hilton Garden Inn spread. There’s also a bar that operates during the week, though it’s closed on weekends, which was a slight letdown for us. The outdoor seating area looked cozy and inviting, perfect for a relaxing moment between adventures.

We also appreciated the gym in the basement, which allowed us to keep up with our fitness routines. Plus, the pantry near the front desk was stocked with all sorts of goodies for snacks and essentials.

The staff were incredibly helpful and attentive, making our stay all the more enjoyable. Given how busy the hotel was, we were impressed by how well they managed everything. They even arranged a car service to pick us up for our flight.

Overall, we truly enjoyed our stay at the Hilton Garden Inn on W. 35th St. and would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a comfortable and conveniently located spot in the heart of Manhattan!

Soggiorno assolutamente deludente. La struttura si presenta antiquata e in condizioni manutentive imbarazzanti. Le camere sono tappezzate da una moquette lurida: considerando quanto siano sporche le strade di New York, su quella superficie si potrebbe tranquillamente avviare uno studio microbiologico per la quantità di batteri presenti.

Il bagno – se così si può chiamare – è minuscolo e, come tristemente consueto fuori dall’Italia, manca del bidet. Questo dettaglio, ogni volta, mi lascia esterrefatto e conferma la sensazione di quanto sia carente l’attenzione all’igiene personale nei Paesi dove il bidet non è previsto.

Il condizionatore della stanza era in condizioni pietose: filtri e alette completamente intasati di polvere, peli, capelli e probabilmente altro che è meglio non sapere. Anche il comodino accanto al letto aveva i cassetti interni coperti da un visibile strato di polvere.

L’armadio? Una vera barzelletta: una misera fessura nel muro con una porticina di legno che si apre a fatica, al cui interno si trova una micro asta su cui è possibile appendere sì e no quattro stampelle.

I letti non sono nemmeno matrimoniali standard, ma da una piazza e mezza, pur trattandosi di una camera venduta come “quadrupla”.

Unica nota positiva: il personale è stato sempre cortese e disponibile. Ma per il resto, se fossi il signor Hilton, mi vergognerei ad associare il mio nome a una struttura del genere.

Nice hotel with slightly larger rooms in NYC at a good value price point.  Location is exceptional for a lot of things.  Staff really makes this place shine, and goes out of the way to make a great experience.  Room had a wonderful view of the Empire State Building.

While the staff wasn't explicitly rude, the front desk team could significantly improve their helpfulness and welcoming attitude. There were several issues that detracted from the overall experience:

1. Room phone functionality: The in-room phone numbers for housekeeping and other services didn't work. If these services aren't available via phone, it would be better to remove the phone or clearly communicate alternative contact methods.

2. Water accessibility: It was inconvenient to have to go to the reception and then be directed to the workout area on a different floor just to get drinking water. The hotel should either provide water in rooms or clearly communicate where guests can easily access it.

3. Digital key issues: When the Hilton app's digital key feature didn't work, the office staff's response to my request for help was condescending and unhelpful. A more patient and solution-oriented approach would have been appreciated.

4. Overall service attitude: The staff seemed indifferent to guests' needs and concerns, which significantly impacted the stay quality.

Due to these issues, particularly the poor service experience, I won't be returning to this location. As a paying customer, I expected a higher standard of service and accommodation.

April 2025 - this is my first stay at this hotel.  The room I had was spacious for an NYC hotel and very comfortable.  There was floor to ceiling windows and given the direction I was facing, the room was very bright.  The room was very clean and the bathroom looked reasonably new.  The room was carpeted.  The hotel has 10 rooms per floor wrapped around 3 elevators - so there might be elevator noise (although I didn't hear it on a high floor)  They are associated with a parking garage where you drop the car off and you can charge the parking to your room (for normal size cars only - not oversized) which is a great convenience for business expense purposes.

The only downside is that the breakfast area is a-la-carte and set up like a Starbucks with grab-and-go items.  You only get $10 as a Diamond credit and they charge $4 for a 12-oz cup of coffee (with 1 refill).

I do not believe that there was coffee available in the lobby.

As strange as it sounds, I have become accustomed to getting hot coffee as I enter or leave hotels and this is a rather big disadvantage for me.  While there is a Keurig in the room, it just isn't the same.

Given this 1 downside, I would consider other Hilton properties - since there are ones nearby that have similar rooms and slightly better amenities (like coffee)

We had to take our own luggage to our rooms, this may be a problem for some people. There was no offer of help but I assume if you ask they may oblige.
I had a double room with an amazing view on the 24th floor of the Empire State Building (I think it was Rm 2408).
The room was an ok size for NY. I would’ve loved a room that was a bit bigger but I assume you would have to pay a lot more for that.
The beds were very comfortable but my pillows kept dropping down the back of the headboard.

I was given a card & a little gift bag for my birthday - Thank you so much, it was greatly appreciated. These little touches mean a lot.

The shower was good and had great water pressure. Pls note that you should turn the shower head towards the wall before turning it on or it will freeze you.
The toilet is massive by UK standards😂

The ac made the room really cold. Excellent if it’s really hot. I was a bit chilly in the evenings & asked for a couple of blankets. At first I was given extra towels & then I was given the blankets that I asked for. Not really a problem just miscommunication.
There was a bit of noise, late at night & early in the morning but you’re in NY, suck it up!😊

House keeping came every day, if you didn’t need them to clean you just had to put a sign on the door.
In the mornings you had to wait a short time for the lifts as maintenance etc were also using the lifts.

I saw a little shop by the side of reception which I used on occasions but I didn’t realise & wasn’t told that they had a breakfast room. I saw it on the day I was leaving as it’s next to the luggage storage. I wish I had known it was there. I definitely would have used it. It looked really nice.
They also do a 1 hour complimentary late check out, if availability is good. Sadly, I wasn’t able to get it.
They organised a taxi when I was leaving. I was told that it was US85 but was charge US95. Not a big problem but prices should be accurate or explained if additional cost may apply.

To summarise- I love your hotel. It’s in a great location with great staff.
As soon as I’ve saved up enough, I will definitely stay with you again.
Thank you Hilton Garden Inn

Great location, but with typical NYC room size limitations
The Hilton Garden Inn on West 35th Street boasts a superb location. It's right across from Macy's Herald Square and near multiple subway lines, making everything in NYC easily accessible. The hotel itself has a well-stocked shop in the reception area, which was a lifesaver for grabbing midnight snacks. The staff was friendly and helpful, providing everything I needed during my stay.
However, it's important to be aware that this is a mid-range hotel with typical New York City room limitations. The rooms are on the smaller side, which is common for the city. Additionally, while there is a microwave, there weren't any microwave-safe dishes available to heat up food. The HVAC unit also lacked a fan-only option, which made it chilly at times since I couldn't adjust it to just circulate air.
Overall, this hotel is a solid choice for budget-minded travelers who prioritize location and convenience over spacious accommodations and high-end amenities.

She came right over and loudly said we couldn’t sit there and they don’t allow dogs. When I explained he wasn’t a pet and was a service dog she looked irritated. At that point I no longer was hungry and was very embarrassed.

This DoubleTree is a modern, well-located hotel in Downtown Jersey City, right on the waterfront across from Lower Manhattan. It’s a popular choice for travelers who want easy access to New York City without paying Manhattan prices. The hotel feels business-oriented but comfortable, with a clean, updated interior and a generally reliable Hilton standard. Guests often choose it for the combination of space, views, and transit convenience, especially because PATH trains and ferries into Manhattan are very close by.

On the third day I received a check-in text from the desk asking how things are and I repeated that we still had no wifi.  No response.  My daughter called support again and spent another half hour on the phone with no result.  The next day I had a text conversation with the front desk.  He claimed he went to our floor and checked empty rooms on either side of us and it worked fine.  (That may be true, but I am dubious.)  He then offered to show me on his phone that the wifi worked.  I complained and asked what good that would do us if his  phone worked.  He finally apologized and offered to comp the expensive $27+tax daily parking fee for our week. After emailing on Monday I had heard nothing by Friday, so I called her and she said it was in process of being refunded.  When it finally was the next week, it was for almost $40 less than what we had originally been billed.  In looking at the invoice I see they had neglected to comp us for one day of parking.  I replied by email explaining this and including screenshots from my text conversation where this was agreed upon.  No answer.  After two weeks I emailed again, including screenshots of the texts, invoices, etc., and got no response.  This is not an inexpensive hotel and to be treated in such a shoddy fashion is unacceptable to me.  Not to mention lack of wifi could be a serious problem for anybody trying to keep in touch with the office, as well as infuriating for teenagers staying in a room for vacation.  Can’t recommend this hotel.

Es ist ein vergleichsweise kleines Hotel, dadurch kein Gefühl von Überfüllung, Gedränge und es gibt nirgends längere Wartezeiten. Die Matratze vom Schlafsofa hingegen leider schon recht durchgelegen. Etwas gestört hatte uns, dass leider keine Fenster zu öffnen waren. Hinter dem Hotel führt eine Straßenbahnlinie entlang. Das Geklingel der Glocke war trotz der an sich sehr guten Isolierverglasung noch hörbar. Aber erträglich. Strassenverkehr kaum wahrnehmbar. Genauso wie das Zimmer selbst sehr gut isoliert ist. Es gab so gut wie keine Geräusche aus Nachbarzimmern zu hören. Diese Ruhe haben wir sehr geschätzt. Das Personal an der Rezeption war stets sehr freundlich und hilfsbereit. Das war wirklich wunderbar. Die Bar abends haben wir 2x genutzt. Dort war nie viel los. Es gibt sicherlich schönere Hotelbars, aber es war alles sehr sauber, die Getränke waren soweit gut, der Service in Ordnung, wenngleich dann aber doch alles auf Manhattan Preisniveau lag.
Wir würden das Hotel wieder buchen, weil wir die Ruhe geschätzt haben, den tollen Blick auf Manhattan und das Preis-Leistungsverhältnis für uns einfach stimmte. Und mit der PATH ist man wirklich schnell in Manhattan.

By 11:00 AM, they had still not arrived. Meanwhile, multiple staff members were gathered in a guest room down the hall, sitting and eating with a cart of towels nearby. I called again to repeat my request and, before leaving for lunch, placed a “Do Not Disturb” sign on my door. This was intentional, as there were prescription medications clearly visible in the room, and I did not want anyone entering while we were out. Despite this, staff entered the room anyway. When we returned, the towels had been placed inside the bathroom. The “Do Not Disturb” sign was visibly hanging on the door handle; there is no reasonable explanation for ignoring it. This is a serious violation of guest privacy and basic hotel standards.

When I contacted the front desk, my concerns were dismissed and I was met with pushback instead of accountability.

To make matters worse, I realized shortly after checkout (within 10 minutes) that I had left a bathing suit hanging in the shower. I called immediately and was later told it was never turned in to lost and found. Given that staff had already entered my room despite the “Do Not Disturb” sign, this is extremely concerning.

At best, this reflects a complete lack of professionalism and protocol. At worst, it raises serious questions about the handling of guests’ personal belongings.

I would strongly recommend management review their staff practices, particularly regarding guest privacy and lost item procedures. I wish that this situation would have been addressed promptly and properly instead of how it was handled.

We usually don’t leave reviews, but we felt it was important to share our experience to help future guests better understand what they might encounter during their stay.

First and foremost, the location was absolutely stunning. We stayed in a room on the 22nd floor, which offered breathtaking views of the river—a definite highlight of our trip.

However, there were several issues that impacted our overall experience:

1. Upon arrival, the staff was unable to locate our reservation, which caused unnecessary stress after a long flight from California. Thankfully, they eventually found it, but the initial confusion was unsettling.

2. The room itself was uncomfortably stuffy, and the air conditioning struggled to maintain a comfortable temperature throughout our stay, making it difficult to relax.

3. Attempts to contact the front desk from our room were frustratingly unsuccessful. No one answered the phone, and messages sent through the app went unanswered. We had to go directly to the front desk to address any concerns.

4. Unfortunately, no room service was provided during our stay. This left us with unmade beds, overflowing trash cans, wet towels on the floor, and empty body wash dispensers, which was disappointing.

5. Even after speaking directly with housekeeping and the front desk, room service was still not provided. Instead, we were handed towels to carry up ourselves and given two small bars of soap as a substitute, which felt inadequate.

6. We were only informed about the credits available to us on the night before checkout, leaving us scrambling to use them last-minute at the gift shop for souvenirs.

Overall, the staff appeared indifferent and not particularly interested in assisting guests. We hope this feedback helps improve the experience for future visitors, as the location truly has so much potential.

Our check‑in experience was just okay. The initial greeting felt a bit cold, almost as if our arrival was an inconvenience. Interestingly, once we asked the front desk associate how their day was going, the tone warmed up noticeably. It wasn’t a bad start, but it certainly wasn’t the welcoming first impression we hoped for.

The room itself was fine, and the beautiful harbor view truly elevated the stay. That view ended up being the highlight of our visit and made the location perfect for everything we had planned.

However, several issues with the room which made the stay less comfortable than expected. Our reservation was for two guests, and while the bathroom had two towels and washcloths, one towel had multiple stains. We called housekeeping for replacements, but after an hour with no update, we called again. The guest services rep said they would follow up, but another 35–40 minutes passed with no response. Eventually, I went down to the front desk, where they acknowledged the request had been shared, they provided towels from the back. Ironically, housekeeping arrived at the front desk to inquire on my request, as the towels were provided. After a long day and a late check‑in, we were simply hoping for a nice shower and rest.

Additional concerns included a heater/air conditioner that didn’t work—the fan never turned on—and a toilet that ran continuously. The shower head’s water setting couldn’t be adjusted, and there were no hand towels provided for initial pre-room service. Hand towels were provided with the first housekeeping clean.  Since we were only staying one more night, we chose not to call in the maintenance issues.

Despite the inconveniences, the harbor view and the hotel’s location were genuinely enjoyable and made exploring the area easy. With some improvements to service responsiveness and room maintenance, this could be a much better experience.

Emad (Manager)

What stood out most was his kindness toward others; seeing him take the time to play with a baby boy at the neighboring table showed his true character. He is full of life, engages in wonderful conversation, and ensures every guest feels seen and cared for. His "encouraging goodbye" was the perfect finishing touch to a stellar meal.

Sian (Security)

As a young woman traveling, I cannot overstate how much I appreciated Sian’s presence. He went above and beyond his "job description" to ensure my safety and comfort. He personally verified that my Uber was the correct vehicle before I stepped in, held every door, and maintained a professional yet incredibly kind demeanor. His consistent "good mornings" and farewells made me feel secure and looked after. His kindness will truly never be forgotten.

James (Server & Bartender)

James is a master of his craft. He was incredibly gracious and possessed deep knowledge of both the dinner and drink menus. His recommendations were spot on! Even more impressive was his attentiveness… even on days when I wasn't dining at the restaurant, James would make a point to greet me and my sister feel welcome. That level of recognition is what separates good service from world-class hospitality.

One of the worst, certainly the most unique, hotel lodging experience of my life. We stayed at this hotel on 12/30/25. At approximately 4:30 AM we, along with everyone else staying in the hotel, were awoken to a blaring alarm/siren with an automated message indicating there had been an emergency on site and to remain on standby. 30-40 minutes go by and the same alarm and message again are blared over the speaker system. After about an hour had passed from the initial alarm and no update had been provided, I called the front desk to see what was happening and was told there was no emergency. A drunk, belligerent guest had set off the alarm and that was all. So we were rattled awake in the early morning for an emergency, which at the time we thought could have been anything, and turned out there was no emergency at all. This was alarming and unnerving. We did not know whether we should evacuate or stay in our room because there was literally no further update or instruction from the hotel management or staff. At no point did the hotel update us, make an announcement that there was no emergency, apologize, or make any attempt to restitute the situation. If we didn’t call the front desk, we would have had no idea what was really happening. To say we were shocked is an understatement. Whoever the standing manager was at the time should be relieved of their duties to their negligence and gross mishandling of the situation. I plan on calling management or Hilton corporate offices after the holiday to lodge formal complaints. Regardless, we will NEVER stay at this hotel again.

I am a Diamond Hilton member and have stayed at this hotel previously without issue. However, I am posting this review because the hotel refuses to acknowledge or rectify the horrible sanitary conditions of the room.

On first glance, the hotel room was lovely with three windows on the corner facing the river. Though dated, that is not a problem.

What is a problem is absolute filthy conditions of the bathroom, towels, and linens & the staff's inability to rectify.

When I arrived, I noticed the bathroom floor was covered in bits of hair - I wiped up what I could and threw in the trash bin. The light switch had grime and brown dirt all over it - I wiped that down as well. The shower curtain was not snapped in properly as the plastic buttons were warped. When I went to shower, the shower head prayed in all directions so I decided to wipe it clean to allow water flow. What I cleaned left moldy bits, water scum, and debris coating the wash cloth. When I grabbed a towel to dry off it was stained orange. Then the second towel had hair strands woven into the fabric. The hand towel I used had weird brown / red stains. The final hand towel is what I used to dry off as it was the ONLY clean linen in the room.

I stayed for two nights and no one came to clean the room despite never putting the 'do not disturb' sign out. When I notified the front desk about ALL the issues - the gentleman was horrified at the photos! He said he would have housekeeping come to clean my room at 8pm when we left for dinner. He provided us with fresh towels but as he handed them off, he noticed the wash cloths had hairs woven into the fabric - he promptly went to get fresh ones.

We came back at 11pm and no one had come to clean the room. The next morning when we use the "fresh" towels, we found those to also have long strands of hair woven in the fabric. We used one towel for two people.

Upon checkout, we told the front desk clerk the entire saga & expressed how truly disappointed we were in the sanitary & cleanliness of our stay. She apologized but offered no compensation or even Hilton points to rectify.

Overall, the staff was kind and listened but that does not negate the overall sanitary conditions of the hotel much less their desire to rectify the issue when notified.

We called the property/front desk as we hit the road from our home in Syracuse NY to let them know we'd be arriving between 9 - 10PM. They said "no problem" and never mentioned anything about the room we booked being unavailable.
We arrived at 9:52PM on the dot but, upon checking in, we were told that the King suite we booked on the 10th floor or higher with a river view was no longer available and that they could put us in a room with two Queen beds instead. My husband and I can ONLY sleep in the same bed if it's a king which is why we go out of our way and put special emphasis on making sure we have a King bed whenever we travel. I calmly tried to talk out this issue with the front desk but Ann Thomas, didn't want to be calm and cordial back. I truly hope that this whole interaction is on video somewhere. I finally was ready to take the L and just go with the room with the 2 Queen beds and just make the most of it. TONS of King rooms were available. Our stay at Hilton Club New York during New Year’s was simply fabulous. The location and comfort exceeded all expectations. Staying for four nights turned out to be the perfect decision, as we were within walking distance of Times Square for the ball drop, iconic shopping destinations like the Louis Vuitton store and Trump Tower, and so much more.

The view from our room was breathtaking. We loved keeping the curtains open just to soak in the stunning sunrise and cityscape—an experience in itself!

The staff deserves special mention for being incredibly friendly and professional. The male reception staff, in particular, stood out—not just for their service but for their undeniable charm. It seemed like looks were a part of the hiring criteria!

Having a separate check-in counter on the 36th floor was a thoughtful touch, helping us avoid the bustling lobby downstairs. Speaking of which, the restaurants in the lobby served some truly delicious meals, and the bar offered fantastic cocktails that added to the overall experience.

At just over $500 per night, it felt worth every penny, especially given the time of year. Rates might be lower during the off-season, but for New Year’s, this was the ideal choice. Highly recommended!

* The rooms are the most spacious of any place I've stayed in NYC by far, and there are plenty of food and drink options in the hotel as well as nearby.

Cons:

* The heat in the room is controlled seasonally so I couldn't turn the AC on. It was probably 72 or warmer in our room even though it was set to 65, and I couldn't sleep without the window open which let in the constant siren sounds and honking. My watch says I got 3 hours of sleep Saturday.

We stayed for a week in a studio room on the 38th floor. Not much of a view except for equally tall buildings all around. Room was reasonably big and bed was comfortable. There's a mini fridge, coffee capsule machine but no kettle or microwave. There're bathrobes but no bedroom slippers. Everything was in good working condition and Wifi was strong. The only negative comment was that we could hear people talking from the next room.
Room was serviced daily and bottled water and coffee capsules  could be replenished on request with no limit. Cleaning and reception staff are friendly and efficient. Checking in & out was quick and efficient.
Lift lobby was very busy and crowded especially when there were corporate events. 1 of the 3 lifts serving my floor has broken down making it worse and resulted in longer wait.
Location is unbeatable, so convenient. But just note that metro station at 7 Ave is not an accessible station and carrying heavy luggages up or down the multiple level and narrow staircase is challenging.
Hilton Honors timeshare sales staff approached us at the lobby multiple times to invite us to their sales pitch but we declined each time. Some days we find that we've to look out for them to avoid them.

The property is perfectly located in the middle of midtown Manhattan and just a few blocks away from central park and about a 12 to 15 minute walk to Time Square. The rooms are the average size for NYC, but the housekeeping staff is excellent. I was very disappointed with the lounge, it's one of the most meh! that I've seen. Don't expect any special acknowledgement or treatment as a Hilton Honors Diamond member, at least I didn't see any difference in the way I was welcomed or my room when compared to non Hilton Honor members. Finally, the elevators are a nightmare. We waited for 8 minutes for an elevator from the 36th floor to the lobby. Overall great location, below average amenities and lounge, and a hit or miss staff.

Very good hotel

Very nice Hilton hotel in midtown. We stayed in the Hilton Club which is in the same structure as the Hilton except the Club floors are higher up. All staff we met with or talked with were very nice. Coffee & Tea (Keurig) are in all rooms for free. There are food options, a couple of bars and Hilton Club executive lounges within. By staying with the Hilton Club, you get its elevated rewards which do come in very handy if you are a not Hilton Club member and can be used throughout Manhattan. The room we stayed in, see pics, was small but efficient. I do wish the bathroom lighting had an additional bright light and there were outlets on both sides of the bed. The outlets issue is something I have noticed at many hotel or resort chains so I'm prepared for it. The only real issue is the elevators. Be prepared to sometimes wait about 5 to 10 minutes or more sometimes to get down to lobby or get up to your room. The causes are heavy traffic up and down and if there is a non-working elevator. Luckily it didn't mess up our stay or delay where we were going. They offer free internet like all Hilton's but if you really need to use it, I recommend paying for the faster connection because the free is not very fast but good enough for basic internet browsing. Lastly we stayed in early June and the humidity builds quickly so be sure to adjust your thermostat to a cool enough temp to rid the extra humidity after showering. Will definitely be returning, but as a Residence member.

Very nice rooms and heated toilets. Views are pretty top notch in some spots and location can’t be beat! Staff was great and very helpful and friendly. Looking forward to next stay.

I’m giving this hotel 5 stars for size and cleanliness. I’ve stayed in plenty of hotels in NYC, and I really appreciated how spacious the rooms are at Hilton Club The Central at 5th. In a city where room size can be hit or miss (mostly miss), this was a huge plus.

They also offer additional amenities upon request, which really came in handy. My wife forgot her toothbrush, and housekeeping delivered a toothbrush, toothpaste, and mouthwash to our room shortly after we called the front desk.

The staff was friendly and accommodating throughout our stay. With a 10AM checkout, it was great that they held our luggage while we ran a few errands before heading out.

Location-wise, it’s perfect if you’re in town for entertainment. We were about a 15-minute drive from Lincoln Center’s David H. Koch Theater, a 12-minute walk to the Lunt-Fontanne Theater, walking distance to Times Square and 5th Ave shopping, and just a 3-minute walk to the train (we used the orange line’s F train).

I had an incredibly disappointing experience during my stay at this Hilton Club The Central at 5th New York property, which is particularly disheartening as a Diamond member who rarely shares negative feedback.

While the hotel itself was decent — especially the lobby bathrooms (all the bathrooms are equipped with bidets) — my experience was marred by significant issues. Upon arrival, I was greeted by buckets placed throughout the lobby, catching water from a leaking ceiling. This unexpected situation led to a halt in check-ins. Although we waited two hours for the water to be restored, we soon discovered that there was no hot water available for our showers. For two days, my family and I endured freezing cold showers, despite being repeatedly assured that the issue was resolved and they were waiting for the boilers to heat up. I was visiting NYC with my family but my daughter fell very ill after taking 2 freezing cold showers so we were stuck in this dungeon for most of the trip and had to order medicine to help her recover. The lack of communication from the staff was particularly frustrating; we only learned about the hot water problem by stepping into the cold shower ourselves.

On the third day, hot water returned, but it was scalding hot — so much so that it felt like boiling water. To add to our difficulties, the elevator broke down later that same day. When it came time to check out on the fourth day, I had to make three phone calls just to receive a copy of my bill via email.

Given these numerous issues, I cannot recommend this property. I will unequivocally refrain from returning, unless points are offered in exchange for this review. I believe my experience warrants a serious reconsideration of management practices at this location.

We have been Hilton Grand Vacation owners for several years but this was the first time using out points to stay in NYC. We were lucky enough to snag a reservation over Labor Day weekend for a One Bedroom Penthouse room. I must say we were impressed! The room was beautiful and with the PH we had a fantastic view! Everyone mentions the heated toilet seat and bidet and of course I must too! What a nice added feature! But we also really enjoyed the power blinds and even the "Master" switch at the door so you don't have to turn everything off individually before you leave the room. Plus, especially for NYC, it was spacious! We had plenty of room for two adults and two kids.

The staff was friendly and helpful as well. On the first night when we realized we did not have bedding for the sofa bed, we called to the front desk and not only did they send it right up, but they sent someone up to make up the bed for us. This was super helpful and so much nicer than the normal quick knock and dum of a pile of bedding into your arms at most hotels.

The only reason I did not give 5 stars is for two reasons. First is that we arrived pretty early in the day (around 9am) and, as expected, our room was not close to ready. No problem, we expected that. We checked in, left our bags will bell services and off we went to explore. When we came back around 3:45pm with two highly exhausted girls, however, our room was STILL not ready. Of course, check in is not until 4:00pm so technically it was not late, but I guess I figured since we had been there so early that perhaps our room could have bene prioritized? This is something I don't understand about hotels. They will have rooms cleaned that sit empty for hours until the guests arrive while in some other cases, the guests are there early and they are made to wait. So we had to wait in their interior courtyard (which was very cute) and our room was FINALLY ready at 4:10pm.

Second reason for 4 stars (versus 5) relates to trash pickup. Staying here as an owner, we do not get daily housekeeping, which we are fine with. But on the second day, I texted down to the front desk to ask what we should do with our trash that was piling up. I was told to just leave it outside our room. So we put it out there, assuming it would be picked up soon. 24 hours later it was STILL sitting outside our room in the hallway. For such a nice place, this just seems a bit unseemly. So they could have been better about that.

Overall, it's probably nit picking. We really did have a great time and would stay there again. Location is fantastic, rooms are nice and staff is exceptional (if they could just be better about trash). I would definitely recommend giving this place a try.

The Hilton Hotel on 5th Ave takes pride at its military mentality - security circles the place 24/7, making sure that people carry their Hilton key cards. You cannot get to your room or inside of the hotel after 10:00 pm, if you don’t have the Hilton key card. Even then, you are asked by staff members who are you, occupation, which room you are staying in, if you are with family, boyfriend and so on. I happen to fall asleep twice on the hotel sofas in the hang-out lobby, because I study and work arduously almost all the time and I tend to become exhausted easily. The two times I was woken up and questioned by the security guards as to who I was, got escorted with security back to my room, the security guards wanted to see my room and who I was with there. Asked to see a key. I showed them but it wasn’t enough. They still pushed to see my room. Very uncomfortable, no privacy. Security told my fellow colleagues that they couldn’t study, nor work in the lounge and eat at the same time. MILITARY MENTALITY AT ITS FINEST. People are well-behaved on the service side, while they collect information about you and comment on everything that you do in terms of how many times you come in and out. Being a journalist I tend to pay close attention to behavioral patterns and the Hilton team crosses many privacy boundaries. Very clean place - they have cleaning team that does this for hours overnight. The food places are great inside as well as the different shops. You are highly judged on appearance and status at this hotel. Comments were made with absolutely no tact or remorse; but again the amenities and the rooms are wonderful. Staff changes daily and the communication between them is not as efficient but still somewhat productive. I enjoyed my stay and I laughed at being woken up and escorted to my room. It is definitely not the best hotel, not one of the best hotels that I have been to. Something about the security guards is highly disturbing and the way they interact with people is frightening and intimidating. They don’t care if you are a woman, they will mistreat you and put you down, or ask you to leave if they decide that you don’t comply to their unwritten rules. The Central at 5th Hilton Club is grand at the price range. Everything is on point from guest interaction, to features, to cleanliness. It exceeds expectations for a quick getaway experience. My check for cleanliness starts with the on/off letters on light switches (the Central uses toggle switches), and there is no grim or dirt anywhere to be found. Everything is featuristic such as the alarm clock that is integrated with the phone and charges the TV remote (also plugs with USB and thunderbolt). There are plenty of outlets, so you shouldn't need a power strip. There are even connections for USB-C on the desk. You also don't need to bring your own media, they offer easy to use streaming connections to Netflix, Amazon, etc. The rooms are NY sized, but has lots of storage so there is no clutter. There is no restaurant on sight so you have to plan, but there are so many nearby food choices that you have efficient options. There are a few other lux features such as a bidet, and automatic blackout shades, but I'll leave the rest for you to explore...

First and foremost, if I were rating purely based on the staff and the location this would be a 5 star review.  The staff are incredible.  They're friendly.  They're helpful.  They really go above and beyond with every single interaction.  You can't beat the location.  It's a few minutes walk from St. Patrick's and Rockefeller Square.  The rooms are spacious and beautiful.  If you don't actually want to spend any time in your room other than to snap a few pictures of the view, it's great.  At the end of the day though, the rooms are uncomfortable.  Everything was clearly designed for style not comfort.  It's the little things that ruin the experience.  For example, there's no way to reach your towel from the shower.  You either have to lay your bath towel in the floor or step out of the shower wet to get to your towel.  If you step out of the shower wet and drench the bathmat, there's no where to hang the bathmat to let it dry.  The thermostat doesn't go any lower than 68 which is great for the environment.  However, the HVAC lets the temperature creep up to 70 degrees before the a/c kicks in to cool it back down.  Given that the ideal sleeping temperature is about 65 degrees, when it gets up to 70 in the middle of the night you're hot.  The furniture is still and uncomfortable to sit on.  While I wouldn't refuse to stay here again, I certainly wouldn't seek it out.

The hotel bar was just okay—my wife ordered a margarita that came out as a basic version instead of the special, but we didn’t make an issue of it. Additional costs add up as well: $10 for overnight parking and $22 per person for breakfast, which didn’t feel like it justified the price compared to other nearby hotels where breakfast is included.

Overall, it’s a nice place, but a bit too expensive for what you get and some of the inconveniences you have to deal with.

The staff here are very friendly and welcoming, which made for a pleasant check-in experience. The location is convenient, right off the interstate and close to a large shopping mall, so it’s easy to find and great for quick access to stores or restaurants.

However, a few things could be improved. It’s surprising that there’s no free parking, especially since this isn’t a downtown or airport hotel. The parking fee is only $10, but it still feels unnecessary for the setting, and the free spots that do exist are quite limited and often difficult to find. Additionally, there’s no executive lounge, which might disappoint frequent travelers or business guests used to that amenity.

Overall, it’s a comfortable and well-located hotel with excellent staff, but the parking situation and lack of certain perks make it feel a bit less convenient than expected.

Leider gibt es aber auch einige Punkte, die den Gesamteindruck trüben. Das größte Manko ist die Hellhörigkeit der Zimmer. Man hört nicht nur die Geräusche vom Flur und den Nachbarzimmern, sondern teils auch Schritte von oben oder Gespräche von nebenan – wer einen leichten Schlaf hat, wird hier keine erholsame Nacht verbringen. Auch Türen fallen recht laut ins Schloss, was gerade nachts störend sein kann.

Insgesamt ist das Hilton Christiana ein ordentliches Hotel mit gutem Service und angenehmem Ambiente, allerdings mit deutlichen Schwächen in Sachen Schallschutz. Wer keine geräuschempfindliche Nachtruhe braucht, dürfte sich hier dennoch wohlfühlen.

When my first reservation was done, I had wrote on the app, and asked if I would be changing rooms (packing up and out for the day at work), or staying in my current room. I didn't like the answer, and shared my displeasure with Caleb from the front desk.

Mind you, I'm exhausted, at my wits end, and fresh off of 4 weeks of being sick from the latest round of whatever cold is out there, so I'm looking forward to push back due to my "karen" like behavior. Without missing a best, Caleb replied professionally and succinctly. I noticed I had failed to maintain my lane and apologized to him,  to which he replied it wasn't necessary.  I woke to a note under my door, saying in essence that he understood tough weeks happen, and wishing me a better week, along with a beverage credit.

Now, I know this is long winded, but I wanted ensure that it's understood that he was professional, above and beyond. The true face and representation of the Hilton Spirit, that I've found to be missing in all too many establishments. I suggest that Hilton finds a way to encourage him to explore advancement within their organization.

In regards to the actual Hotel, it's rooms are on the smaller side, many Without a worthwhile view. The beds, pillows and linens are perfect however.

It's located in an extremely convenient area, with many options around for food and entertainment. If you get the urge, there are several hiking trails within 20 minutes of here.

If you eat at the on site restaurant,  the menu is pretty typically priced for an on site establishment,  but the food is much better than most. I'd suggest the truffle fries, but avoid the grease laden cow burger. The staff there is very attentive, and you won't feel as though you're ignored.

The breakfast, it's a bit steep, and as much as I'd like to put a positive spin to it, I'm drawing a blank on how to make it seem value worthy, convenient, or tasty.

Overall, this is a great location, with staff that goes above and beyond. To the point of changing your view on your entire week.  I'd suggest them for any meetings, showers, reunions, or even vacation goers.

Our stay at Hilton Wilmington Christiana had its ups and downs. Upon arrival, we were greeted warmly by the reception staff and promptly checked in (even a little early), which set a positive tone for our stay. The hotel offered a pleasant ambiance; the common areas were clean, and the lobby was well-appointed. Parking was ample and the location was convenient, offering easy access to local attractions, shopping areas, and the venue where we attended a wedding during our stay.

Our initial room appeared well-maintained and comfortable. We did request a room with a lanai, which was lovely, but a room next to the pool can be noisy. The real problem arose when we attempted to take a shower. The water temperature fluctuated constantly, oscillating between scalding and freezing. We contacted the front desk twice, and they were quick to respond. However, the first technician insisted that the water was at a consistent temperature, and we simply didn’t let the water warm up long enough. On the second call, another technician named Stephen validated our complaint and offered us a room change without hesitation. It is always an inconvenience to have to switch rooms in the middle of a stay, but the second room did provide a respite from the inconsistent water temperatures, albeit now we had to endure the traffic noise from the other side of the hotel.

Despite this issue, the hotel staff remained friendly and attentive, and the other aspects of our stay were satisfactory. We also really enjoyed the Market Bar and Restaurant. My only other complaint would be that for the price (nearly $800 for a 3-night stay), breakfast should have been included. Overall, it has potential, but improvements are needed to justify the cost.

I stayed at the Hilton Garden Inn on Park Avenue and found it to be a well-located, comfortable base for exploring Manhattan. It’s just steps from Grand Central Station, Times Square, Bryant Park, and several subway lines, ideal for getting around the city with ease.

While it’s not a luxury hotel, it delivers solid comfort and convenience. The room was compact (typical for Manhattan), but thoughtfully arranged with a very comfortable bed. There was a faint musty scent, possibly from humidity or age, but nothing that disrupted our stay. The overall vibe was cozy and functional.

The front desk staff were genuinely kind and welcoming, which made a big difference. Housekeeping was reliable, and the spacious lobby offered a nice place to relax. There’s also a restaurant attached to the hotel, which adds to the convenience.

For the price point, it offers great value, especially considering the location and service. If you're looking for a well-situated, comfortable place to stay in Manhattan without splurging, this hotel is a smart pick.

Excellent Hotel within the city.....

In 25 years of travel, this is the best hotel I have found in midtown. The rooms are very modern and cozy but not cramped. There is a very generous desk space for working and a quality chair. The staff are great and will offer help for what you need before you even have to ask. The thing that really sets this hotel apart is how it has been brilliantly designed for noise reduction. In most   NYC hotels, you will experience some street noise during the night from garbage trucks, sirens, and even loud music from the rickshaws. I’m not sure what architect designed this hotel but they were a genius. Many room windows face the inside courtyard of the block rather than the street. Between that and the think windows, you can sleep without nose. I don’t even hear noise from the hallway which may be in part due to the thick doors they use. It is an oasis of tranquility in a busy city where you can get restful sleep. On Friday night, my friend around the block, at another hotel we keep awake by noise, and I never heard a thing. The near perfect noise reduction and level of quiet here is extraordinary and something I have not experienced in another Manhattan hotel .

I lived in NYC for 20 years so like to think I have realistic expectations that hotels in the city will be overpriced and underwhelming (unless you're spending significant $$). That said, there is such a thing as cheap and cheerful. This place bummed me out for several reasons: the room was dated, had bad feng shui (small is to be expected but the flow was off) and the biggest issue was lack of sound proofing. Beyond the occasional neighbor & street sounds, my room had sounds coming from the floor above that sounded like intermittent banging of pipes or a door slamming in the breeze-- maybe the heating ducts or? either way,  it was a repetitive, never ever ending sound that was disruptive and not relaxing). Also, my "city view" room stared straight out at a tall brick building (when I asked about this, they told me anything above the 20th floor is automatically considered a "city view"--so if you pay for this upgrade, maybe be specific in your ask). Pros were: reception staff were pretty nice, the room was clean and the bathroom was modern with good water pressure. Overall though, this room had a sad, blah feel and I couldn't wait to get out of it! Would not return.

If you are a solo traveler I would get something closer to actual time square like NYC Marriott marquis. My first two nights were at that hotel and it was much better

Elegimos este hotel para nuestras vacaciones en NY. Lo mejor, sin duda alguna, la ubicación. Permite ir andando o a lo sumo en metro (a escasos metros del hotel) a casi todos los puntos de interés.
Casi todas las personas del hotel han sido muy simpáticas y atentas.
Lo malo. Las habitaciones y las instalaciones en general necesitan una renovación. Al menos en nuestra habitación había muchos ruidos. Algunos derivados de la ventilación y, en fin de semana, de una terraza del hotel en el que montan unas fiestas importantes los fines de semana.
En general recomendable pero me ha parecido un poco feo el detalle final de cobrarte por guardarte las maletas un par de horas el dia que dejas el hotel. Con lo que cuesta este hotel se podían ahorrar esos 2$ por maleta (las dos primeras gratis).

We were very satisfied of our stay until we checked out and discovered the policy for luggage storage: we were charged $2 per extra luggage (the first 2 are free)… We were pretty surprised by this policy (first time an hotel ever applied it to us) and it was not a great experience to discover this (pretty unusual) practice at the last minute, especially when we were expecting to share our satisfaction with them.
In addition, when we shared our surprise to be charged for carry-on luggages and backpacks (no jumbo suitcases!), the only embarrassed response we received was that it was a usual practice in NYC… Again, not our first visit in NYC… It could have been so elegant to waive these costs, even if they are now part of the hotel policy.

When we first walked into the room, we saw the staff had decorated it for my birthday with balloons and streamers! How cool is that?! The staff also signed a happy birthday card for me. These little details are so appreciated and helped make our stay even better.

Whenever we needed anything (i.e. more towels, toilet paper, sheets changed, etc.) it was done quickly and everything was done by the time we returned to our room.

The only thing that would’ve made this review 5 stars would be different pillows in the room, as we all had some very flat and uncomfortable pillows and the beds weren’t the best, either. But it worked for the time being!

The restaurant attached to the hotel is expensive but the food is tasty.

We will definitely be staying here again if we come to NYC!!

2. The room had a king size bed that took over the entire room. I only had one midsized piece of luggage and I was tripping all over myself because there was no space in the room.

3. The bathroom door wouldn't stay open because it is too close to the room entrance door, so for safety reasons, the door always closes shut. I had to prop the door open with the trash can just to get rid of steam fogging the mirror after a shower and to get some ventilation.

The Hilton Garden Inn New York/Midtown Park Ave is a stylish hotel located in the heart of Midtown Manhattan, just a short walk from the Empire State Building, Macy's, Fifth Avenue, and Times Square. It is the ideal base for business and leisure travelers alike, offering easy access to all that New York City has to offer.

The hotel features oversized guest rooms with contemporary amenities, including complimentary WiFi, an ergonomic desk, a 32-inch HDTV, a walk-in shower, a Keurig® coffee maker, a microwave, and a mini-refrigerator. Four spacious terrace rooms are also available, offering wonderful views of NYC.

Guests can enjoy a variety of delicious dining options at the hotel, including the Ainsworth Midtown restaurant, which serves breakfast, lunch, and dinner. The hotel also has a 24-hour business center and a fitness center, so guests can stay productive and active during their stay.

Whether you are in New York City for business or pleasure, the Hilton Garden Inn New York/Midtown Park Ave is the perfect place to stay. With its convenient location, comfortable accommodations, and thoughtful amenities, the hotel offers everything you need for a great stay.

During my recent stay at the hotel, I encountered a few aspects that left me with mixed feelings. Firstly, the AC in the room was set at a fixed temperature of 74 degrees, and unfortunately, it couldn't be adjusted to a colder setting. As someone who prefers a cooler environment, this was a disappointment. It's important for hotels to cater to varying temperature preferences to ensure optimal comfort for all guests.

In addition to the AC issue, the room itself felt somewhat dated. While it provided the basic amenities needed for a comfortable stay, the overall decor and furnishings could benefit from a modern touch to enhance the ambiance and create a more inviting atmosphere.

Moving on to the conference room, which happened to be located in the basement, it too shared the same dated aesthetic as the guest room. Furthermore, the lighting in the conference room was noticeably low, creating a somewhat dim and less productive environment. Considering its basement location, introducing natural lighting or improving the overall lighting system would greatly enhance the functionality and appeal of the conference room.

On a positive note, I must commend the staff for their pleasant and accommodating demeanor. Their friendly and helpful nature added a positive touch to my overall experience at the hotel. Their professionalism and willingness to assist guests are commendable qualities that significantly contribute to creating a welcoming atmosphere.

In summary, while there were certain areas that could benefit from improvement, such as the inability to adjust the room temperature, dated room decor, and lackluster conference room lighting, the hotel staff's pleasant demeanor served as a redeeming factor. It's crucial for hotels to continuously evaluate and update their facilities to meet the evolving needs and expectations of their guests.

If you prioritize a cool room temperature, a modern ambiance, and well-lit conference spaces, it may be worth considering alternative options. However, if friendly and helpful staff is a priority for you, this hotel might still provide a satisfactory stay. Ultimately, it's important for hotels to address these areas for improvement to ensure a more enjoyable experience for their guests.

Frustrating Disorganization & Poor Communication – Lost Items Mishandled

I recently stayed here from April 10th - April 12th with a group of students, and the hotel’s handling of students lost items was shockingly disorganized and mishandled. The staff failed to follow basic protocols. When I contacted the hotel to arrange return shipping, I was told to provide a prepaid label (unlike other locations in the same chain that handle this seamlessly). Even after sending a shipping label and clear instructions, the hotel:
- Lost track of a confirmed item (an employee verified glasses were found, yet they weren’t shipped).
- Ignored follow-up emails and calls, requiring repeated explanations to staff.
- Shipped only partial items without true acknowledgment or apology of the missing item.

What should have been a simple resolution turned into days of wasted time and frustration. The lack of accountability from both front desk staff and management is unacceptable, especially for a hotel chain under the Hilton name. If you stay here, triple-check your room—and don’t expect help if anything is left behind.

I recently stayed at the DoubleTree by Hilton Hotel Fort Lee - George Washington Bridge, and I strongly advise against staying here. This was one of the most unpleasant hotel experiences I’ve had, and I’m shocked this property carries the Hilton name.

The room was outdated, dark, and depressing—nothing like the photos or the 4-star rating it claims. It felt like stepping into a hotel frozen in time, with minimal effort to maintain or upgrade the space.

The worst part was the smell. From the moment I entered, there was a lingering, sickening odor that only intensified when I tried to use the heater. The smell made me physically ill. Turning off the heater didn’t solve the problem; the odor remained, and I was left freezing in the room. There was no way to feel comfortable.

The towels were disgusting, clearly not properly washed or replaced, and smelled foul—almost as if they had been reused from a previous guest.

To make matters worse, I could hear conversations from other guests during the late night hours (around 2-3 a.m.), making it impossible to get restful sleep.

Adding insult to injury, the in-room phone didn’t work, which meant that any time I needed something, I had to physically go downstairs to speak to the front desk. This is completely unacceptable for any hotel, let alone one branded as Hilton.

I cannot stress this enough—this hotel is a disaster. Until it undergoes a major overhaul, avoid it at all costs. It does not live up to the Hilton standard and is absolutely not worth the price.

After an 18-hour flight, I arrived exhausted and sick with the flu, just needing a clean bed. The room itself was clean and comfortable — but that’s where the positives end.

From the start, the staff seemed entirely money-driven. They kept inventing new charges, roughly $180 in total, including a “short stay” fee that I had already paid. Out of sheer fatigue, I just complied.

The manager, Ronald, had no understanding of the Hilton Grand Vacations (HGVC) programme and was unhelpful. When he finally decided to “assist,” he took my credit card and disappeared into a back room for over 30 minutes without explanation. I also witnessed him berating another guest who was occupying the penthouse in a rough and unprofessional tone — shocking behavior for a Hilton manager.

While I was visibly unwell and coughing (wearing a mask they provided), a female front desk employee began threatening not to check me in because I was coughing — showing zero care or compassion.

To make things worse, I had three packages delivered to the hotel. They initially gave me two and insisted that was all until I pressed them to look again — only then did they find the third package, after disputing that it was even delivered.

At checkout, no bellmen were available to help with luggage. I dragged my own suitcases down, and no one behind the counter even acknowledged me.

Terrible, unkind, and unprofessional service from start to finish. The focus here is on squeezing guests for money, not treating them with basic decency. I will never return.

Original Review:

The hotel charges a $20 cancellation fee even when canceling a month in advance.  This was associated with Hilton Grand Vacations, where they try to sell you timeshares.  Note that I had already paid them $285, which I was not trying to get refunded.  I need a timeshare pitch even less than a hole in the head, but it's not their fault I agreed to it.  Rebooking was also a $20 fee.  It has been a long time since a business has made me this furious.  Note that they are pulling this stunt with Honors members with status, so they are doing this to their best customers.

If you get a call from Hilton Grand Vacations, don't listen, don't give a credit card and hang up.  I hope this negative review costs them many times the money I paid. Nice room and bathroom. Great location. Hospitable staff.

Close to Time Square, about 3/4 of a mile, and Central Park is two blocks away. Eateries all around, including great pizza.  Same block as Carnegie Hall and Russian Red Tea House!

The location of the Hilton Club West 57 TH street is very good. First and foremost, in relation to the wonderful Central Park, but also in relation to e.g. subway, short distance to shopping on 5 TH Avenue, for example. The room (we had a "King Studio") on the 14th floor with a view of 57th street, was also very good. We were very happy with the room itself. Very good and comfortable beds including pillows and mattresses. Everything was clean and nice, as well as a great bathroom in addition. As I see it, the cleaning staff did the best job and gave the best impression. They were both good and nice, all credit to them.

Another advantage of this hotel was that there were never problems with crowded elevators, slow elevators and long waits for elevators, as you often find in more traditional hotels in NYC. However, the lobby area was not particularly inviting and pleasant. The lobby area was small and cramped and no seating where it was possible to sit secluded to relax or to do a little work. But what was most disappointing about the hotel was the general level of service and customer service from the reception staff. My assessment is that the level of this important element is that it was far below what I expect and what I am used to. I have stayed at other more traditional hotels nearby and further down towards Times Square, where the level of service was of a slightly different caliber. Not least, I have stayed at other Hilton hotels, especially on the west coast, where the customer service was at a significantly higher level. It started already at check-in, where they initially were unable to find my booking (this was sorted out relatively quickly). When I, as politely as I could, asked if it was possible to get a "nice room a little higher up the floors", I received answer that "all our rooms are nice". I mean, when you are going to stay at the hotel for a week and pay a significant amount of money for just that: it must be possible to at least try to be a little nice to the guests? How difficult is it to say, “thank you for choosing to stay here with us, have a wonderful week here at Hilton Club”?

A little more focus on customer service and maybe a few more hours on basic staff training, would hardly hurt. Another small challenge that I experienced was about my wish for a late check-out on the day of departure. We are talking about 3-4 hours extra time beyond the normal check-out time. This was anything but easy, even though I of course made it clear that I would pay for these extra hours. I also reffered to the hotel's own policies, where it was written in black and white that late check-out is possible. But anyway, this was sorted out in a good way in the end. But not without the hotel manager having to decide about it. The manager was nice and as mentioned, this was resolved. But I think this is one of very few times in my long hotel career (as a guest) that I have experienced late check-out as a real problem.

But to summarize: the room and location were great, the cleaning staff were both nice and good, but beyond that I feel that Hilton Club West 57 TH is lacking to much in other areas. Therefore, it is important to be aware that Hilton Club is basically a slightly different concept than what you may be used to from other, more traditional hotels. This is important to be aware of BEFORE you book this hotel. I for one had not done my homework here, I had not done enough research beforehand. Therefore, there will probably be no return to the Hilton Club on my next trip to NYC, unless the hotel management convinces me otherwise.

I bought a Hilton Grand Vacations time share here 3 years ago, and I've never been sorry. Love the location - steps away from Central Park & Carnegie Hall, and within easy reach of multiple subway lines. For owners, there's free breakfast & heavy appetizers, beer, and wine from 5-7. For me, that's dinner, and all the food is catered from a nearby & very tasty restaurant. If you don't own at this property, those meals won't be available to you, but there are many restaurants within easy walking distance. Staff have always been friendly, and the area very convenient. There's a Duane Reade on the east side and a wonderful grocery store a few doors west, and as stated, Carnegie Hall on the same block, at 57th & 7th Ave. This property is closer to the 6th Ave side. There's also a gymn, if you don't get enough exercise walking. For me, walking 3-5 miles a day is easy in the city. All in all,  it isn't cheap, but it's a great stay!

I stayed at the Hilton Club West 57th in NYC. I was very impressed. I did attend an informative session regarding ownership. I did not purchase at this time due to conflicting priorities, however I was satisfied with the information shared and it’s still a consideration for a later time. I loved that this building was located on Billionaire’s row in Manhattan. It’s within walking distance to many attractions like Carnegie Hall, many luxury stores, the Museum of Modern Art, Central Park, and many places to eat.
The building is very well kept and everyone was friendly. I did have use of the gym facility and it had a wide range of equipment.
My room was on the 18th floor and had great views from the floor to ceiling windows.

This review may not be applicable for everyone, but I think it is important to share because others may have (or be able to avoid) a similar experience.

My partner and I were considering purchasing a timeshare with Hilton. We spoke with someone when we were staying in Hawaii, and purchased a travel plan to see how the program works. We were told by the person who sold the program to us (and received a commission) that the timing could be flexible and not to worry about the expiration on the contract because customer service could extend the time for us easily. I was concerned because the timing of expiration on the contract aligned with when I was scheduled to take the bar examination after completing law school. We really wanted this trip as a celebration for completing law school and the bar. I vocalized this to the agent and they assured me it would not be a problem. However, once the contract was signed, and we spoke with the customer service agents over the phone (no longer the people trying to sell us something) we were told, rather coldly that we would have to book the trip before the expiration time or forfeit the money.

We were forced to book a whirlwind trip here in February, weeks before I am scheduled to take the bar examination, or lose the money we contributed to this "program." We were forced to go to New York because this program only allows for a three day trip at this one location (even though they advertise dozens of other locations). We previously stayed in Cabo for our first trip on this "program," which allows for a total of 7 days collectively for the credit purchased. We stayed in Cabo for four days and only had three days left under our program. Then we were told we could only stay in New York because that is the ONLY location that allows for three day stays. Absolute scam.

We were (AND ARE) extremely upset with Hilton and their abysmal customer service, but decided to make the most of the trip rather than forfeit the money we already spent. However, it was clear upon arrival that we were not high on the priority list. We already had our "pitch" regarding the possibility of purchasing a timeshare in Cabo, which we declined. Our second stay in New York had no pitch scheduled and once the staff realized we were not a possible commission, the service became laughable.

Also, we came when it was not busy, yet somehow ended up in probably one of the worst rooms possible, a courtyard room on the fourth floor, right next to the elevator. Also, this "view" of the court yard is an extremely generous statement. Upon checking in, I asked about the rooftop courtyard, and the receptionist responded, "that's for owners with a penthouse only," and made me feel like I was an idiot for even asking. Even though there are dozens of pictures on these reviews and the website showing a rooftop courtyard that looks public. So do not expect to be looking out at the city like in their picture advertisements, the view from our stay will likely be more indicative of your future experience.

Needless to say, we will not be returning.

I spent 7 days at this hotel and received world class service. Shout out to Sheila (reception) and Avi (door man). They were so gracious and wonderful that we want to take them on our next vacation.

Complicated, inconsistent, irritating at best is how I would describe the new IT system they have installed for streaming.  If you happen to not like a movie or series, you can't just make a new choice. You get bounced out, have to reboot, and register again, over and over.  The algorithm does not remember you, so every single time you want to make a change, you must start all over. !!!!  :(
Simplicity is the key to happiness here, and with the average age being 60 plus who own here, a revision of this brand new system is already in order, or this property will fall in the overall ratings.
But other than that (which is a big deal which is why I am noting it in this review), we absolutely LOVE everything about this extraordinary property. Kudos to not only the management, but every single person who works here...by far the most amazing staff in NYC, hands down.
Thank you for always making it feel like we have come home when we visit. We will be returning for decades to come!!!

First, upon check-in, they told me my reservation wasn't in their system.
I gave them my Expedia confirmation details. They took about 10 minutes to sort it out, and I was finally given the room key.
No explanation or apology on their part.
Second, the bathroom sink faucet was not secured to the countertop. It wobbled and was about to come apart when I turned it on or off.
The handle motion was also very rigid, making the on/off motion difficult (room 2101).
Why would housekeeping not report this to be fixed?
Finally, I was locked out of my room because the door's electronic lock malfunctioned!
The maintenance guy was confused by the problem and took nearly 2 hours to finally open the door.
Evidently the whole mechanism had to be replaced and it was not just an expired battery problem, or a faulty keycard.
In the meantime, I asked for and received a different room, and was able to retrieve my belongings from the original room, when it was finally accessible.
Upon check-out, I asked for one-night's comp, and they readily agreed.
But there was no apology from them. Hospitality and accommodation are not qualities of this hotel.
I was so glad to leave as quickly as possible.

Location is nice, across from the Staten Island Ferry, but with the construction out front, we couldn't park to unload the luggage! I backed in and unloaded at the front door. Then I had to drive around the block to park in the garage - and walk back to the hotel.

L’environnement est top, c’est calme et très facile d’accès en transport. L’hôtel est calme, propre et le personnel très accueillant, merci notamment à Martin, Heidi. Kay et Alyssa !

This room should have been saved for someone else who needs it. Honestly though no one should be using it until things are better and fixed. The shower had black mold in the corner caulking. The shower head, even with adjusting, was subpar. Hot water was scolding hot or barely tolerable or cold. I was curious to why there was a stack of 3 extra bath towels on the bed when I got to the room. Now I know why. The shower was a transition-less, flat entry for ADA reasons and it’s understandable given that’s the purpose of the room. However water did not slope and drain to stay in the slippery shower floor (not ADA compliant). No, the water slipped out and into the overall bathroom floor. Sure there was an extra drain hole in the bathroom floor near the shower entry (so 2 drain holes), but it didn’t actually drain. The slope is not acceptable for either drain. I had to use all 3 of those extra towels to mop up the excess bathroom floor water. Seriously what in the world — yeah, not ADA compliant. I’m blessed to not have a physical disability but if I did and traveling alone there would be seriously dangerous possibilities using this bathroom. I will never stay at this hotel again for this reason.

The Hilton Garden Inn in Lower Manhattan is a great hotel and I would absolutely stay here again. The location is right across the street from the Staten Island Ferry and a 15-ish minute walk to the pathway across the Brooklyn Bridge. It is a skip over to so many cool spots in Lower Manhattan. The room was quiet, had all the things I needed (incl great water pressure!). Elevators were fast enough and I never really had to worry about waiting too long (all times of the day). There is a huge building blocking the center of most of the hotel views, but you get awesome views of the water on either side of it. I opted for a water view room and attached quite a few photos of the view from the 16th floor spot I was in.

*It is worth noting that the bathroom door is not a door, but instead a sliding wooden door that doesn't necessarily give you complete privacy.
*The window opens inward a few inches and has a sun shade in addition to the big, dark curtain.
*Room had a mini fridge and micro. Fridge did NOT have a freezer.
*Had an iron, no ironing board.
