There’s nothing remotely 5-star about this hotel. The only redeeming part of my stay was the breakfast team, who were genuinely kind and professional — they’re the only reason this review isn’t a single star.

The rest of the property is in serious need of maintenance. The bathrooms are a disaster — drains don’t work, toilet flushes are broken, and murky water actually comes out of the taps. It’s unacceptable for a hotel that markets itself as “luxury.”

Front desk service was polite but unreliable. There’s little regard for Hilton Gold members or loyalty in general. And if you happen to deal with an agent named Riki, good luck — guests can absolutely tell when staff are whispering or exchanging comments about them. It’s unprofessional and uncomfortable.

Overall, this hotel completely misses the mark on what a Hilton 5-star experience should be. I would not stay here again or recommend it to anyone expecting quality, transparency, or basic functionality.

During my recent stay at the Hilton Dubai Creek Hotel & Residence I was impressed from the very beginning.  Before arriving I received a pleasant welcome email providing helpful information about the property and its facilities which I found useful.  On arrival I was greeted by the friendly team in the unusual parking area entrance which felt a little different but worked well.

I made my way to reception on the ground floor and was again welcomed and supported by the team.  Check in was quick and efficient and before long I was on my way to my room.  On arrival I was pleasantly surprised to find a lovely apartment style room with a nice balcony overlooking the creek and hotel pool.  The apartment included a kitchen and dining area, a separate bedroom and a separate bathroom and toilet with all the usual amenities.

The property offers the usual Hilton facilities including a nice restaurant, Shisha bar, spa, gym and a relaxing pool area with a bar available too.  There was also a happy hour in the restaurant for Hilton loyalty members which was a nice touch.  It is worth noting the hotel is just over a year old and the unique entrance  can create some confusion for taxi drivers.  I found that walking to the hotel next door helped as it has a more traditional hotel entrance which made it easier for drivers to locate.

The residence is part of a lovely development on the creekside and the newly opened supermarket close by was ideal for picking up essentials or for a simple meal in the apartment.  The location is convenient for both the airport and the original parts of Dubai which makes it a great base for exploring the city.

Overall I really enjoyed my stay, the team were helpful throughout and the property was always clean and well maintained.  I look forward to visiting again in the future.

Riki was very helpful and attended to all our needs, marwa and suren made our stay very welcoming as well, Definitely recommend this hotel

The spa was amazing, room services are quick n convenient and especially Mr. Riki from the reception took our stay to a whole another level of comfort! Along with Riki, Marwa and Suren have been extremely helpful too. So polite and humble and hospitable, made the whole experience even more worth it!! Best of luck to them!

My Review of Hilton Dubai Creek

I stayed at Hilton Dubai Creek for two nights, from October 26 to 28, booked directly through Hilton. I had planned to extend my stay until October 30, but I couldn’t because the service was very poor.

From the moment I arrived, the experience was disappointing. I had mentioned in my booking that it was my birthday, but no one paid attention. When I reminded the staff, they just said, “Okay, tomorrow when you check out and the room is cleaned, please call us so we can put a greeting card for you.” This response was careless and made me feel completely unappreciated.

The room itself was old and worn out. We even found a cockroach in the bed! The room smelled strongly of cigarettes, even though I had specifically requested a non-smoking room.

Overall, it was a terrible and unpleasant stay. I will not return to this hotel again — a very disappointing experience.

As much as I want to give it five stars, I have to drop it down a little bit because the rooms themselves did have a few issues: one of our rooms didn't have hot water, and another had the doors to the bedroom and bathroom that would lock themselves (so neither the bathroom or bed were accessible at first).

Also, the hotel itself is a little awkward to get to right now as the Jewel of the Creek tunnel puts you into an underground carpark which only a fraction of taxi drivers are familiar with. Though I do believe this might be less of an issue with time, as we stayed during all of the construction work going on around the Jewel of the Creek, so nothing was walkable from the hotel and taxis were required for everything.

Going back to the good points: I do want to reiterate that the staff were all excellent and eager to please. We all enjoyed the breakfast -- I really loved the shakshuka especially. We also did have a late lunch at the hotel restaurant where the portions were massive.

I recently had the pleasure of staying at Hilton Dubai Creek Residences, and I cannot speak highly enough about the incredible experience we had from start to finish.
From the moment we arrived, the level of service was nothing short of outstanding. The reception team set the tone for our stay — I would like to give a special thank you to Ms. Meriem and Ms. Marwa, who welcomed us with genuine warmth, efficiency, and professionalism. Their attention to detail and thoughtful assistance made us feel valued and truly at home.
To our delight, they also surprised us with a complimentary upgrade from a 1-bedroom apartment to a beautiful 2-bedroom residence. The suite was spacious, elegantly designed, and offered panoramic views of the iconic Dubai Creek and the stunning Burj skyline. Waking up to that view is truly magical!
Our dining experience was equally memorable. We had the pleasure of dining at Serein Restaurant, where we were graciously looked after by Shayne, whose service was impeccable. Shayne’s friendliness, professionalism, and genuine passion for hospitality added a personal touch that made every meal special. The food was excellent, with a variety of dishes that catered to both local and international tastes.
The hotel itself is modern, spotless, and thoughtfully maintained, with all the amenities one could need for a comfortable, relaxing, and luxurious stay. Whether you’re visiting Dubai for business or leisure, Hilton Dubai Creek Residences offers the perfect blend of comfort, service, and location.
To the entire team — thank you for making our stay so memorable. We are already looking forward to returning and will be recommending your property to friends, family, and colleagues without hesitation.

4 months later said friend has received another fine of an inflated price, with no response to any of our emails, phone calls regarding the ‘investigation’.
This is such a shame because we had a great stay in Leeds but this completely ruined the experience for us and has caused nothing but disappointment about our time away still 4 months later and I advise all to be wary about staying here if you’re planning on driving as it is not worth it.

Found someone’s ball sack hair on the bed, was dirty, like it wasn’t clean

Absolute nightmare to deal with had a reservation and had to cancel due to unforeseen circumstances, tried to rearrange was declined, tried to cancel that was declined, had two wasted emails that wasn’t responded to, and two phone calls that were completely useless and waste of time the call handlers weren’t helpful whatsoever.

Unfortunately, the price-performance ratio was very poor. We are Gold members and had to carry our suitcases up a very, very steep staircase right at the beginning, which was quite inconvenient.

The room was very old and in need of renovation. Instead of proper heating, there was only a space heater. The chairs were almost falling apart, and the overall interior felt very careless and uninviting.

The bathroom was even worse: there was mold, half of the lights didn’t work, and there was rust. The bathtub-shower combination was very cramped, and there was hardly any space to put personal belongings. We were three people in a four-person room, yet only one person could store a few things in the bathroom.

Breakfast was also disappointing. There was very little variety, and the coffee machine took a long time to make a single coffee, which led to long queues. There were a few vegan yogurts available, but unfortunately they were already gone the next day.

In general, the hotel’s interior and bar area were not very appealing.

The only positives were the location of the hotel and the staff, who were all very friendly.

However, we paid an extremely high price (200 per night) for what felt more like a motel room. For that reason, we will not be returning.

Don’t be misled by the fact that this hotel carries the Hilton name or its star rating. Setting aside the location, even staying for one night was a genuinely unpleasant experience.

The bathroom was in poor condition the shower curtain was visibly dirty, and there was no basic hygiene kit. We even asked for a simple cotton pad and were told they didn’t have any (which makes you wonder what would happen in a basic first-aid situation).

Leaving that aside, there wasn’t even a bottle of water in the room. We woke up in the morning knowing checkout was at 12, yet not even a small bottle of water was provided until then. At the very least, a glass could have been offered so we could drink tap water.

I absolutely do not recommend this hotel. I have stayed at many Hilton properties over the years, and this is the first time I’ve encountered such a disappointing standard.

Hilton York, 1 Tower Street

Perfect location for a York stay. Walk around the side of the hotel and you’re right in the city centre: JORVIK Viking Centre is about 100 metres away, Fairfax House is roughly 50 metres, and Clifford’s Tower is practically on the hotel’s doorstep. You’re also surrounded by plenty of bars, restaurants, and shops.

Our room was very well appointed and spotless, with a modern-classic feel throughout the hotel. It’s clearly well maintained, and our room’s view of Clifford’s Tower was amazing. The bar is modern and comfortable, and breakfast was a strong highlight—an extensive buffet plus a chef making eggs to order.

Parking-wise, pay for the on-site valet. There’s no self-parking at the hotel, and the nearby Castle car park is expensive, so valet is the easiest, best-value option.

Service was good overall—friendly and helpful—but not quite excellent; it could be a touch sharper and more responsive at times. That said, everything we needed was handled without fuss.

Overall, highly recommended. We visit York a few times each year and would definitely stay here again for the unbeatable location, comfortable rooms, and solid amenities.

Stayed here for three nights to do the tourist thing in York. The hotel is well located, a very short walk from everything the city has to offer. Walking from the train station is also quite easy, about 20 minutes. The room was very clean and comfortable, and quiet. Plenty of space in the room, although the bathroom was a little cramped - but not a problem. Plenty of power outlets near the bed for charging, and the sockets had both usb-a and usb-c sockets on them, the first time I have seen this, a very handy feature. The breakfast buffet was wonderful, with views out over the castle across the street. The staff could not have been more helpful when I asked to change rooms - they were the best of any Hilton I have stayed in in the UK. Highly recommended.

If you are an American Hilton Honors member, here is an honest review for you:
-Book out way ahead and do not book non-refundable through the Hilton Honors app. They will not make an. exception under any circumstances, even if you want to spend more money at hotel.
-No executive lounge or perks besides breakfast. Breakfast is lovely here but when bringing food to my teen daughter in room, they stopped me and said you can’t bring food out. Weird. They said it was because if anything happened with the food they are at fault? Even more weird.
-Don’t expect to be treated special in any way for being a Hilton Honors member. This goes for all of UK Hiltons. They are mostly inflexible and really into adhering to policy. Not sure if it’s a cultural thing but it’s not customer service focused in any way. They didn’t ask if I wanted an upgrade and I had to remind them breakfast is “free” with gold member status.
-The location is really awesome. Easy access to everything there is to do and see. There are fans in the wardrobe for those of us who need white noise to sleep.
-Peloton subscription not on bike. You can’t log in and use your account. Isn’t that a perk of Hilton hotel gyms?
-Overpriced.

Hilton here is just very different from Hilton experiences in the states. I’d say that you could stay at Premier Inns and skip the high cost of these properties, which is what I’m doing in Scotland. I think you’d be just as happy. I say this as a loyal Hilton customer. Though I’d also say the luxury experience with this brand is slowly dying.

I chose the DoubleTree by Hilton Birmingham Perimeter Park because I typically trust the Hilton brand. This stay did not meet even basic expectations. From the moment we arrived, the condition of the property raised concerns. The parking lot was visibly unkempt, with trash present, and there is a posted sign stating “park at your own risk” with no liability for loss or damage. At the same time, guests are required to pay for parking. Charging for parking without any visible security, patrol, or controlled access is difficult to justify. During check-in, I accidentally left my wallet at the front desk. After getting to our room, I realized it was missing and called the front desk multiple times with no answer. We went back downstairs, and the clerk confirmed he had my wallet. While I was relieved it was there, what followed was unacceptable. He had my wallet, knew exactly who I was and what room I was in, yet made no effort to contact me. I then had to ask him directly if he saw the incoming calls. His response was that he did see them and chose not to answer. There was no explanation and no urgency. Given the lack of communication, I had no choice but to assume the worst and immediately cancel my cards to protect myself. That added unnecessary stress and disruption to what should have been a routine check-in experience. We were also given incorrect information at check-in regarding breakfast. We were told it was included, but after eating on our second morning, we were informed it was not. To her credit, a staff member stepped in and handled the situation professionally. That was the only moment during our stay that reflected the level of service I expect from Hilton. The room itself showed signs of poor maintenance, including visible stains on the carpet. The TV connection was weird. Always disconnected from the service and multiple remotes. Additionally, there was a repeated police calls to the property, which was alarming and added to an already uncomfortable environment. Between the condition of the property, the lack of security despite paid parking, and the handling of a lost wallet, this stay fell well below expectations. I expect better standards, communication, and accountability from a Hilton-branded hotel. I would not stay at this location again, and I hope management takes this seriously and addresses both property conditions and staff.

3 out of 5 stars – Disappointed for the Price Point

I really wanted to enjoy my stay here, but there were several issues that made the experience frustrating.

Let’s start with the biggest problems:
•	We were never informed at check-in that housekeeping wasn’t standard. Even after multiple requests for daily room cleaning, no one ever came.
•	We never received new towels — we had to physically go to the front desk to get them.
•	The TV volume was awful — I suspect the equalizer was broken.
•	None of the ice machines worked on any floor we tried.
•	I paid for the breakfast package, yet every morning I had to prove I had it to a visibly annoyed attendant. There was no guest list available to make this easier.
•	The buffet area had a leak during breakfast  not what you’d expect from a Hilton property.
•	The lobby snacks were wildly overpriced $5 for a single Hershey bar is excessive.

•      The room didn’t have a microwave!

•	The front desk staff seemed undertrained. They were often confused about how to fulfill basic requests.
•	A room service cart sat outside our door every day, which wouldn’t be a big deal if our room was ever serviced.
•	I requested a luggage rack, and it never came.
•	Our trash was never taken out. I ended up taking the full bins myself to staff and asking them to replace the liners

Some good things about my stay.

The room was clean upon check-in, which I appreciated, and we got a free warm cookie . The location is great close to restaurants and just a short drive to downtown.

But overall, the lack of follow-through, poor service coordination, and subpar experience for the price really took away from what could’ve been a pleasant stay.

This hotel has potential but it’s clear there are operational issues that need serious attention.

My husband and I were in town for one day, near the beginning of May on a cloudy and rainy day around 65 degrees outside. Overall we are happy with the hotel and would stay again, but there are certainly pros and cons.

I prefer to sleep in total darkness. We all know that is impossible at hotels. I appreciated the blackout curtains in the room, but in typical hotel fashion, they let in light around the perimeter. There are many lights outside around the exterior of the hotel and parking lot. There is a nonfunctioning mini fridge behind a cabinet below the TV. There is a functioning mini fridge on the floor underneath the desk. The fridge door is glass that you can see through. There is a light inside the fridge that doesn't turn off. I folded a bath towel in half and draped it over the fridge. It still looked like a glowing neon light in the room all night. If I was staying a second night, I would unplug the fridge.

I can hear the TV in the room next to mine, so the sound proofing could use some improvement but overall it's not too loud. The room decor and furnishings are updated. The room looks nice. Everything is clean and smells fresh. The bed is fine but the pillows are highly uncomfortable. They flatten down and become hard. I like the coffee maker. It makes 2 cups at a time.

The chairs on the balcony are plastic and coated in yellow pollen. I would recommend that the hotel clean the chairs on a regular basis, or replace with chairs that don't collect and show the pollen. A good option would be the chairs that are the thick mesh-y type material.

The parking lot wraps partially around the hotel so you shouldn't have a problem finding a parking spot near your room. The hotel does have a sprawling design.

The pool was difficult to find. There are 2 ways to access. The simplest way is to find a door that leads to the hotel courtyard and then you'll see the pool. There is an outdoor pool and outdoor hot tub. The pool felt cold; I don't think it's heated. The hot tub was unbearably hot. There is also an indoor pool and indoor hot tub. The indoor pool was a comfortable temperature and felt like it was slightly heated. The hot tub was warm. It was an appropriate temperature without being too hot. The outdoor and indoor pools are basically next to each other. The gym is connected to the indoor pool. There is an elevator past the gym where you can go up and get back to your room without being outside. We recently made a side trip to Birmingham on our way to the Gulf of Mexico, as for many years I’ve wanted to visit the 16th Street Baptist Church and the surrounding historic area. We chose to stay at The Kelly and could not have been more pleased with our experience.

From the moment we arrived, we were warmly greeted and made to feel genuinely welcome. Every staff member we encountered—the doormen, front desk, manager, and chef—was friendly, professional, and went out of their way to make our stay comfortable. It truly felt a bit like being at home.

Our room was spotless, with a very comfortable bed and pillows, a nice bathroom, and a large smart TV. We also loved that the building retains the charm and character of the original bank in its renovations—it gives the hotel a unique and elegant atmosphere.

The location is excellent as well: downtown, within walking distance of several restaurants, bars, and shops, which made exploring the city really convenient.

We will definitely stay at The Kelly again the next time we’re in Birmingham. It has our highest recommendation.

stayed at the Hilton in Alpharetta for a work trip and overall had a solid experience with just a few areas that could use improvement.

I arrived late—as usual—and check-in went smoothly. The front desk agent was extremely polite and helpful. I asked for a plastic key (I always do this as a backup in case the app fails), and while she was processing it, another employee (Not front desk maybe engineering are maintenance) chimed in to say I didn’t need to visit the desk if I checked in on the app. I had literally just explained why I came down, so that felt a bit dismissive. Either way, I got my keys (she gave me two) and headed up. My Hilton Gold status wasn’t acknowledged—not a big deal, but it’s usually part of the routine.

My room was on the 7th floor and looked great. Just inside the door was a Cuisinart coffee maker (dual-cup), a small refrigerator with two complimentary water bottles, and a low countertop with two drawers. Between the coffee area and the closet was a wall mirror. The closet itself had hangers, an ironing board, iron, and a safe. At the top of the closet, there was what looked like a concealed compartment—unclear what it's for, but it stood out.

The bedroom had a wall-mounted TV above a second set of drawers with an ice bucket and glasses, a desk with a non-rolling alligator print chair (surprisingly stylish), and a plush armchair with a side table and lamp. Bedside setup included two lamps, an alarm clock, and a limited number of USB ports and plugs. Outlets were scarce overall—one near the coffee maker (but half-blocked), and one in the hallway.

The bathroom was clean, with a glass shower, lighted mirror, and a built-in nightlight (which I always appreciate). Towels were average—not luxury, but not thin or scratchy. A hair dryer was provided. The door had a solid privacy guard at the bottom.

I didn’t have much of a view at night, but the room felt comfortable and well-kept.

Breakfast was a bit of a mixed bag. The staff was great—super friendly and welcoming—but the food didn’t impress. The bacon was practically inedible, hard like plastic. Pork sausage links were decent, and the Gouda cheese grits were flavorful but salty. Pre-made waffles were good. There were the usual extras like breads, pastries, and fruit, but overall it didn’t feel like a standout Hilton breakfast. I didn't go back after that for the rest of my stay.

The snack shop by the front desk could use serious attention. It was heavily stocked with healthy options that didn’t seem to be selling, while candy and chocolate were gone almost immediately. I had a late night chocolate craving and came up empty-handed—only ice cream was available. On top of that, they sell glass Coke bottles that require a bottle opener, but there’s none in the room or the shop. I had to go back to the front desk and ask the guy to use the one he carries in his pocket. That shouldn’t be how it works—either include a bottle opener in the room or offer one with the purchase.

I didn’t request housekeeping during my stay, but I liked that they left a card on my door letting me know I could reach out by 2 PM if I needed anything. That’s a nice, considerate touch I haven’t seen at other Hiltons.

Parking is mostly in the rear. There are Diamond and handicap spots up front, but for most guests, you’ll need to walk a bit either to the front entrance or the side door—even from the closest spot in the main lot.

Each evening, the hotel had a lively vibe. The bar and patio areas had groups hanging out, enjoying drinks and conversation. It seems like a fun place to stay if you're here for leisure. I was just in town for work, so I didn’t get to take advantage of it—and I never even saw the pool (if they have one).

The night staff were friendly and attentive every time I passed through the lobby.

Overall, the hotel is clean, the staff is great, and the room was comfortable. Just a few tweaks—better breakfast execution, a stocked snack shop, and accessible bottle openers—would elevate the stay.

NOTICE: DoubleTree Roswell Formal Legal Complaint For Racial Discrimination and Severe Service Failures at DoubleTree by Hilton

To Whom It May Concern,

I am compelled to formally report a disturbing and blatantly discriminatory incident at the DoubleTree by Hilton, which occurred on Saturday, April 13th at approximately 10:00 AM. This complaint outlines multiple violations of the Civil Rights Act, as well as breaches of consumer protection laws, and announces my intention to seek full legal remedies.

Upon my attempted check-in, I was immediately informed by hotel staff that despite numerous prior instances across Hilton properties, the use of debit cards was now inexplicably prohibited. This abrupt policy change forced me to utilize a less preferred payment method, disrupting my arrangements and setting a precedent for the subsequent prejudicial treatment I received.

As a Silver Member of the Hilton Honors program, I am entitled to certain benefits, including the option of late checkout. This privilege was categorically denied without reason, further indicative of the unequal treatment I endured compared to other guests. This denial is particularly egregious as it directly contravenes the loyalty program's terms, to which I have faithfully adhered.

The situation escalated significantly when I, having checked in online due to the initial payment issue, was overtly ignored by the check-in staff. They prioritized other guests who arrived later, a neglect that persisted for over 35 minutes. This act of sidelining not only demonstrates a breakdown in service protocol but also suggests a pattern of discriminatory behavior based on my race.

In an attempt to resolve these issues, I contacted the front desk only to be put on hold by an employee named Candice, who stated, “I’m not going to have time to deal with you because I have other customers in front of me,” before abandoning the call for an unacceptable duration of 45 minutes. This statement, coupled with her failure to return to the call, exemplifies a clear disdain for customer service obligations and possibly reflects racial biases in customer prioritization.

The encounter reached a critical point when I attempted to escalate my concerns to a higher authority by taking a business card for the dual general manager, Oz Cagdas. Candice aggressively attempted to snatch the card from my hand, directing me instead to contact a lesser authority, Mario, thereby obstructing my access to rightful management intervention.

This series of events, recorded on video, captures not only the explicit rudeness and abrasive conduct of the staff but also their concerted efforts to prevent me from reporting this misconduct. The footage will serve as key evidence in forthcoming legal proceedings aimed at addressing these civil rights violations.

Given the gravity of this incident, which compelled me to immediately vacate the premises for my own dignity, I demand a comprehensive investigation by Hilton into this matter, along with immediate corrective action against the employees involved. I am prepared to initiate a lawsuit seeking not only compensatory damages for the direct and indirect costs incurred but also punitive damages to deter such discriminatory practices in the future.

A response is expected within five business days, detailing the steps Hilton will take to rectify this situation. Failure to respond appropriately will result in further legal actions, which may include but are not limited to, seeking judicial enforcement of my rights under applicable anti-discrimination laws.

I've stayed at this location multiple times when I'm in town for business over the last few years and its better than most in the area. But the issues I've experienced consistently here during most of/all of my stays shouldnt happen at this price point.

Every time I've stayed here, Ive had issues with either the water pressure, the water temperature or both. Ive stayed in different rooms and even different style room (single king, double queen, etc), and I've had the same problem. The rooms use a waterfall type shower head and it seems like the only thing getting the water out of the head is gravity, and its just kind of falling on you. The temp issue also seems to be random, as most mornings I had hot water, but if I showered in the afternoon/early evening I wouldnt.

Something I've also dealt with on multiple floors is humming/whirring noises coming from the interior walls/hallway. Its most likely a ventilation system but its running constantly and can be difficult to drown out. Just something to keep in mind.

This is to be expected for this location, but prices on absolutely everything here are outrageous. Garage parking is $70 a night. Cocktails start at $18. Snacks at the store in the lobby are insane. A bottle of water cost $10. To deal with upcharges like this when paying over $300 a night for a room is kind of ridiculous.

As far as the good goes though, the beds have always been comfortable. And the times I've stayed multiple nights, housekeeping has always been great at keeping everything fresh. The hotel staff in general is always great all around. The breakfast buffet is also good (wish it was included in the cost of the room, but the extra fee is worth it). The location is great too. There was a small charge made of $28.20 after checking out. This charge was for food/beverages on night 2 of of a 2 night stay. There should have been 2 Food/Beverage credits on our room - 1 for each night - and each should have been for $30 per night (2 people, $15 per person per night). Instead, only a single night was credited for the $30 and the second night was not credited.

We assumed that this would be a very easy matter to get corrected, however we were mistaken. We contacted the hotel every day for 6 days, leaving messages that were not returned. Finally somebody at the hotel provided us with an email address and asked us to email the manager that deals with these things (initials MB - Accounts Receivable). We did so and heard nothing at all. After 8 days of silence we again emailed, and then called. This was unsuccessful. We spoke to Hilton corporate customer service who apologized immediately, confirmed that there should have been a credit for each night and the amount should definitely not have been paid and that they would escalate it.

Only then did we receive an email from "MB", and that email was to refuse to process the refund as he believed the credit should only have been for one night. (He also incorrectly suggested that the 1x$15 credit for the second person was a "courtesy credit" - it is a published guaranteed benefit of status in the Hilton chain).

Review: The Starling Atlanta Midtown – Great Location, Disappointing Rooms

The only real selling point of The Starling is its location. It’s right in the middle of Midtown — walkable to Piedmont Park, Colony Square, and plenty of restaurants and bars. If you want convenience, it has that.

Beyond that, it falls apart.

The rooms are poorly designed and feel neglected. The décor tries to be bold and modern but comes off mismatched and cheaply executed. Layout choices don’t make sense, finishes feel worn, and the overall impression is dated rather than stylish.

Cleanliness is the bigger issue. The room did not feel properly cleaned — dust, stained surfaces, and a general lack of attention to detail. Bathrooms in particular felt grimy rather than fresh. For a hotel at this price point, that’s unacceptable.

The lobby and marketing suggest an upscale experience. The rooms do not deliver it.

If location is your only priority, it works. If you care about thoughtful design, cleanliness, and overall quality, there are better options in Midtown.

Nicole was great! Very attentive and sweet! The breakfast was also great!

Not the luxury or boutique hotel it’s trying to be. Hope the meeting moves on to another hotel.

My husband and I drove in from Knoxville, Tennessee for a birthday weekend getaway, and without question, this was one of the most memorable birthday experiences I have ever had. The reason is simple. The people.

From the moment we arrived, the service was exceptional. Our valet, Enoch, unloaded our entire car, brought everything up to the room, hung our jackets in the closet, placed our belongings neatly in the bedroom, and was incredibly accommodating. It set the tone immediately.

I am extremely disappointed with how my cancellation was handled and the level of unprofessionalism I experienced from this hotel.

I spent the entire day trying to call the front desk to cancel a reservation due to a COVID related illness. Each time, the phone was answered and then immediately disconnected. After multiple attempts, my call was eventually rerouted to a customer service center, which finally connected me with a manager named Venice.

Unfortunately, the interaction with Venice was unacceptable. She refused to provide even the first initial of her last name, was rude and argumentative, and showed no empathy whatsoever. When I explained that my husband had tested positive for COVID using an at-home test, she demanded proof in order to issue a refund. When I explained that there was no official documentation to provide, she compared my situation to her needing proof to call out of work which was inappropriate and completely unreasonable.

Long story short: My reservation had been canceled by the hotel.

The man at the front desk was rude, unhelpful,  and clearly either unaware of the hotel systems or, frankly and most likely, being dishonest.   (He kept insisting that I must have canceled my own reservation .... First,  If I did, then I wouldn't have been standing there . We stayed here for one night while attending the SIAC Championship game, and I can't say enough about the outstanding service we received.

Mike, the Front Desk Manager, went above and beyond to make us feel welcome and provided top-notch service no matter how many times we needed assistance. The rest of the staff was just as friendly and accommodating throughout our stay.

We had a minor issue with the key reader on our door, but within 10 minutes, a maintenance employee was on-site and got it fixed - very impressive!

We also spent some time at the lobby bar, where the bartender was fantastic. He was super cool, engaging, and friendly, even while serving our large group, which made the experience all the more enjoyable.

* Skin Care: The rooms come equipped with Neutrogena skin care products, which is a nice touch for anyone who appreciates quality toiletries.

* Complimentary Coffee: There's a complimentary coffee station in the lobby, which is perfect for a quick morning pick-me-up.

* Room Features: Each room is well-appointed with a small fridge, Keurig coffee maker, and microwave, making it easy to store and prepare snacks or meals.

* Convenience: The hotel is easily accessible from I-459, making it a great choice for anyone traveling through the area. Plus, you get a complimentary USA Today to catch up on the news.

* Breakfast Options: There's a cold breakfast for just $5.95, or you can opt for a made-to-order hot breakfast for $11.95 - both are convenient and reasonably priced.

*Individually wrapped food, regular and alcoholic beverages, and personal essentials are located in the lobby's Mini-Market.

* Bar and Patio: The hotel has a small bar offering beer, wine, and a limited selection of other alcoholic drinks. There's also a nice, albeit small, covered outdoor patio next to the bar and dining area with a flat-screen TV, perfect for relaxing or watching a game.

*Indoor Swimming Pool and Hot Tub.

The rooms and guest services were great - no complaints at all. The hotel is also conveniently located just a few blocks from Publix, Starbucks, and several fast-casual eateries and retail stores, which made it easy to grab anything we needed.

Overall, we had a great experience. This hotel offers a solid combination of comfort, value, and convenience. I'd stay here again if I were in the area!

Thanks again, Mike, for your amazing customer service and hospitality.

The lobby is bright and fresh with several open areas for meetings, sitting to have a drink or meal (bar service only) or working.

The rooms are updated but simple. There was a lot of dark hair on the shower walls and a few in the bed, but the sheets looked and smelled clean so I just noted it. The rest of the room was clean.

There is no in room dining available. The only food options on the property is a breakfast buffet which at the time of this writing is $11 per person and is the standard fare of scrambled eggs, potatoes, bacon or sausage and a variety of breads, muffins and cereals. It is available from 7a-10a. Dinner is served at the bar only between 5pm-10pm with a small selection of options. To order you must go down, order from the bartender then wait for them to prepare your meal. You can order it to go and take it to your room.

Upon checking in and arriving on the third floor, we immediately noticed a strong odor of marijuana and cigarette smoke throughout the hallway. When we first entered our room, there was no noticeable smell, and we were grateful for that. The following day, we were away attending a work conference and spent very little time in the room. However, on the second night, the odor had become significantly more noticeable, not only in the hallway but also inside our room.

Initially, we chose not to raise a complaint, as we were focused on our work obligations and assumed the issue would resolve itself. To our surprise, we were later charged a $300 smoking fee, with the explanation that “hidden smoke detectors” had been triggered in our room. This accusation is deeply concerning, as both my coworker and I are non-smokers and did not engage in any activity that would warrant such a charge.

When we attempted to address the situation with staff, specifically an individual named Michael, we were met with a lack of professionalism and courtesy. Rather than working toward a resolution, we were dismissed, accused of being untruthful, and spoken to in a manner that was both inappropriate and disrespectful.

In addition to the smoking fee, we were also charged an extra $220 deposit, which we were told would be refunded after checkout, as we caused no damage and left the room in the same condition it was found. Given that we spent minimal time in the room and used it solely for sleeping, these charges are especially frustrating.

Overall, this experience was extremely disappointing. Being wrongly accused, charged unfairly, and treated disrespectfully by staff has left a lasting negative impression. Unfortunately, due to this situation, we will not be returning.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Beaux-Arts Glamour Meets Modern Luxury in Downtown ATL
If you want to experience the "Gatsby" side of Atlanta, The Candler Hotel is an absolute masterpiece. Originally built in 1906 by Coca-Cola founder Asa Griggs Candler, this historic skyscraper has been meticulously restored into a boutique hotel that feels like a step back in time—but with all the 5-star modern comforts.
The Architecture & Vibe
The moment you walk into the lobby, the white Georgia marble, Tiffany glass, and original brass elevators take your breath away. It’s easily one of the most Instagrammable spots in the city. Even if you aren't staying overnight, the grand staircase is worth a visit alone.
Dining at "By George"
Located in what used to be the Central Bank and Trust, the hotel restaurant, By George, is a destination in its own right.
• The Breakfast: The Tres Leches French Toast is legendary.
• The Dinner: Don’t miss the Atlantic Sea Scallops or the Steak and Fries with cognac sauce.
• The Cocktails: The bar program is top-tier. Try a classic martini—the atmosphere practically demands it.
The Rooms
The rooms strike a perfect balance: high ceilings and large windows that flood the space with natural light, paired with sleek, contemporary furnishings. The bathrooms are spacious (shoutout to the luxury robes and designer toiletries) and provide a quiet sanctuary from the hustle of downtown.
Pro-Tips for Your Stay:
1. History Buffs: Ask about the basement vault. It famously housed the secret Coca-Cola formula for years!
2. Location: You are steps away from the Peachtree Center MARTA station, making it easy to get to the airport or Midtown without a car.
3. Valet: Like most downtown hotels, valet is the way to go, but be sure to call for your car 15–20 minutes early during peak hours.
Bottom line: The Candler is more than just a place to sleep; it’s a piece of Atlanta history. Perfect for a romantic staycation or a sophisticated business trip.
#TheCandlerHotel #ExploreATL #DowntownAtlanta #ByGeorgeATL #CurioCollection #AtlantaHistory
.

I stayed at The Candler Hotel in January 2026 as a Hilton Honors Diamond member, booking through American Express Hotel Collection. The hotel itself is stunning—the historic restoration is remarkable, the lobby popcorn is a nice touch, and there were several instances of genuinely excellent customer service during my stay.

However, I experienced significant issues with benefit communication and management follow-up that required over two weeks to resolve.

The Problem:
At check-in, I was explicitly told by front desk staff that my $100 Amex Hotel Collection property credit could be used for valet parking and would stack with my Diamond food & beverage benefits. I relied on this information when incurring charges.
At checkout, I was informed for the first time of an undisclosed hotel policy requiring the Amex credit to be exhausted before Diamond benefits could apply. Additionally, only one of my two daily Diamond F&B credits was applied to my folio. When I raised these issues and requested correction, the front desk declined to adjust the charges, resulting in an $84 balance that would not have existed had the policies been disclosed upfront.

The Bigger Issue:
I emailed the Front Office Manager the same day (as instructed by the front desk) to request review. I received no response for 17 days.

I escalated to Hilton Guest Assistance twice, each time being promised a response within 48 hours. Neither promise was kept.
I contacted American Express Hotel Collection, who called the hotel directly on my behalf. Even they were unable to get the charge reversed.

After 17 days, five emails, two phone calls, filing BBB complaints, and initiating a credit card dispute, the General Manager finally responded and resolved the issue within 24 hours—something that should have happened on Day 1.

Resolution:
To their credit, once senior management became involved, they were apologetic and provided appropriate compensation. The GM personally called to apologize, and Hilton's Executive Office followed up independently.
Recommendation:

The Candler is a beautiful property with genuine potential, but there are serious training gaps around:

Accurate communication of benefit policies at check-in
Management responsiveness to guest concerns
Empowering front desk staff to correct billing errors

Until they fix these training issues, you may wish to avoid the hotel if you are a Diamond member or booking through Amex. I do hope they sort these training issues soon, as the property itself is lovely and, if possible, I would love to make this my main hotel to stay at when I am visiting Atlanta.

The hotel is beautiful, the room was clean and comfortable, and the service was top notch. Highly recommend and would definitely stay again!

You will never be able to tell that the Candler Hotel was once a bank and was the tallest building in the whole of Atlanta. The bank vault housed the original recipe for Coca-Cola which you can visit.

Dwayne was fantastic in meticulously explaining everything about this historic building. Including that the sun would come in and hit the chandelier. The reflection would then hit the tiles and that’s how the tiles got their colour!

The building became a hotel in 2019 which now makes it a grand 119 years old! It has been meticulously kept and very much loved.

The Art Deco theme that runs throughout is exemplary.

Its grandeur in the main foyer becomes apparent when you feast your eyes upon the staircase that dominates the hall with its sweeping curves drawing the eye upwards towards the majestic chandelier (made of Tiffany glass and original from 1906).

The reception area is warm and inviting. Their complimentary Punch should come with a warning as it is delicious and highly addictive! Not to be bypassed.

All of the staff are wonderful and professional. A special mention for Jerome (Bell Captain of Candler) a wonderful gentleman who made my day with his smile and conversation.

Our room (#311) carries out the Art Deco theme. Once again it was immaculate. The huge windows let the light flood in and showed us the hustle and bustle of a city that doesn’t sleep.

It’s also the first hotel ever (that we have stayed at) to have a black makeup towel! Now that’s a genius idea.

There was only an Espresso maker in our room so we called the front desk to ask if there was a kettle as we like our tea very hot. We were informed that the Espresso maker can also be used for hot water (which doesn’t really heat the water for our British cup of tea!). That was the end of the conversation for us. However when we returned after a long day out much to our surprise there was a kettle waiting for us! What a lovely surprise. Thank you.

Would be stay here again? Absolutely! Without a shadow of a doubt…Yes Yes and Yes.

This bank was turned into a hotel in 2019 but due to covid it first year of operation was 2021. This bank turned hotel was a bath house for a point in history also. This place was originally own by the creator of Coke a Cola, John Pemberton. Which has a secret vault downstairs where he used to store the original  Cola formula years and years ago. You can ask the front desk to show you the vault anytime between 8-4 pm. They will also give you a nice tour of the hotel and its history. Beware because this place is a historic landmark this place has two address, make sure you take Auburn Ave NE to the valet parking area also that street is normally crowded with trucks. My family and I recently had two stays at the Hilton Garden Inn Chattanooga/Hamilton Place, chosen for its convenient location as a halfway point on our travels. While the hotel offered some expected amenities, several aspects of our experience left much to be desired, particularly regarding customer service.

Front Desk Experience (1/5 Stars):
Unfortunately, the most consistent and disappointing aspect of both stays was the demeanor of the man working the front desk. On both occasions, his attitude was incredibly uninviting, bordering on rude. During our first stay, when we requested an extra towel (as only three were provided for four guests), I was told to pick it up myself. After a 15-minute wait at the front desk with no one present, my second attempt to retrieve the towel was met with an unhelpful and surly response. For our second visit, I proactively requested an extra towel and two extra pillows at check-in, only to be told that extra pillows might not be available – a surprising and frustrating response from a hotel.

Amenities & Breakfast (2/5 Stars):
The pool was closed during our first stay, which was an inconvenience for a family traveling with children, though not a deal-breaker. It was open during our second visit. The breakfast, which incurs an additional charge, was described as "okay." However, no information or directions regarding breakfast were provided at check-in on either occasion, requiring us to seek out details ourselves.

Room Condition (3/5 Stars):
We ended up in the exact same room for both stays. The room was generally fairly clean. However, there was a noticeable and unpleasant stain on one of the chairs. Given our previous interactions, we opted not to address it with the front desk and simply avoided using the chair.

Customer Service vs. Hotel Promise (1/5 Stars):
The most ironic part of our stay was the presence of posters throughout the hotel assuring guests that staff were "happy to help" with anything needed to make their stay more enjoyable. This statement felt entirely disconnected from our actual experience, where requests were met with reluctance or a poor attitude.

Conclusion:
In summary, if you are looking for an average hotel with a fairly clean room, an "okay" breakfast you pay extra for, and are prepared to be treated like an inconvenience throughout your stay, the Hilton Garden Inn Chattanooga/Hamilton Place might be for you. Given the availability of other similarly priced or cheaper hotels in the area, this Hilton Garden Inn will unfortunately not be my first, second, or even third choice for future stays.

I stayed here as part of a multi-day road trip and was really hoping for a comfortable, welcoming experience. While the room itself was fine and I appreciated the convenience of the location, the customer service—particularly in the breakfast area—was disappointing. I’m a Hilton Honors member, but I’ve now had several stays in the last couple of months that have left me questioning why.

At breakfast, the staff was noticeably rude and unwelcoming. I felt hovered over rather than helped, and when I returned just before closing to grab something quickly for the road, I was abruptly told they were closing—even though food was still out and other guests were around. It wasn’t a polite heads-up; it was said in a way that made me feel like a nuisance. Not the hospitality I expect from Hilton.

Noise was another issue—between the highway and people in the parking lot, it was hard to get a peaceful night’s sleep. Even with the windows shut, I had to turn up the TV to drown out the outside noise.

I work remotely and started my day at 5 AM to get five hours of work done before hitting the road—so I’m not just a casual guest. I may not be in a suit and tie, but that doesn’t mean I deserve to be treated with less respect. Overall, I’m just not impressed, and this marks the third Hilton in recent months that’s missed the mark.

Extremely disappointing experience from Hilton London Metropole.

This property markets itself as a five star hotel, but in execution, it functions as a solid four star at best. Given the premium room rates of $500 to $700 per night, the actual experience fell incredibly short of expectations.

As a Diamond member, the arrival experience was disappointing. There was a lengthy wait at reception with no dedicated lane for top tier loyalty members. The front desk staff appeared aloof, exhausted, and entirely disengaged, providing a remarkably cold and uninviting welcome. Furthermore, there was no welcome note or standard recognition of status upon entering the room.

The room itself was acceptable, offering a minor upgrade to a high floor with a balcony, but preparation was entirely overlooked. Despite spending three hours coordinating via the Hilton app chat prior to arrival, none of my basic requests for extra pillows, water, or coffee were fulfilled. The communication on the app was slow, unhelpful, and ultimately resulted in empty promises. Having to repeatedly request these basic items after check in turned the stay into unnecessary work.

The Executive Lounge was functional but highly underwhelming, suffering from overcrowding and a very basic selection of food and beverages. Ironically, I have experienced far more sincere, gracious hospitality at three star properties over the past year.

While the stay ended without further incident, the overall lack of warmth and poor execution means I will not be returning.

For travelers looking for a genuine, true luxury experience in London, I strongly advise considering actual five star hotels nearby, such as The Wellesley Knightsbridge. The contrast in staff professionalism, room quality, and overall execution makes it clear that this Hilton property simply does not belong in the same luxury category.

Extremely disappointing experience associated with the London Hilton on Park Lane on St George’s Day.

My party and I had spent the afternoon enjoying a formal luncheon nearby before deciding to visit Shanghai Me within the hotel for drinks. We were all smartly dressed in suits, wearing St George’s member's ties and red roses in respectful celebration of the national day.

Despite being appropriately dressed and behaving respectfully, we were refused entry. The situation left us feeling unfairly judged purely for wearing tasteful national symbols associated with St George’s Day.

What made matters worse was the handling of the complaint afterwards. Different explanations were given, including claims that we had been offered the opportunity to remove items of clothing, which simply did not happen. The final response ultimately relied on “management discretion” while avoiding any meaningful acknowledgement of how the experience came across from a customer perspective.

I fully appreciate that private venues have dress codes and reserve the right to refuse entry. However, there is a significant difference between enforcing standards and making guests feel unwelcome because of respectful expressions of national identity.

Most disappointing of all was Hilton corporate distancing itself from the matter entirely despite the venue operating within one of its flagship London hotels.

For a hotel and venue positioning themselves as luxury hospitality brands, I expected far better communication, professionalism, and customer care.

🏨 What it is (simple definition)

A classic 5-star luxury hotel run by Hilton, sitting on Park Lane in Mayfair, overlooking Hyde Park—one of the most prestigious hotel addresses in London.

📍 Location (major advantage)
Right on Park Lane, Mayfair
Facing Hyde Park
Walking distance to:
Buckingham Palace
Green Park
Oxford Street shopping
Extremely central for sightseeing and business travel
🏛️ Scale & style
Opened in 1963 as Hilton’s first UK hotel
One of London’s early modern skyscraper hotels
~28 floors, tall city views over London skyline
Style: modern corporate luxury + classic Hilton comfort, not boutique or ultra-trendy
🛏️ Rooms & experience
Around 453 rooms + 56 suites
High floors = best views (Hyde Park / skyline)
Rooms are:
Comfortable and spacious for central London
Recently refreshed in many areas
Suites often include access to Executive Lounge (breakfast + drinks)
🍽️ Dining & bars
Multiple restaurants and bars inside
Popular highlights:
Park Corner Brasserie (modern British dining)
Revery Bar (cocktails & lounge vibe)
Strong focus on convenience rather than Michelin-style exclusivity
✨ What makes it special
🔥 One of London’s most famous Hilton flagship properties
🌳 Direct Hyde Park views (rare in central London hotels)
🏙️ High-rise skyline views (great at night)
🚶 Perfect base for first-time London visitors
👍 Why people choose it
Excellent central location
Reliable international 5-star service
Good Hilton Honors benefits (upgrades/lounges)
Easy access to major landmarks
⚠️ Things to know
Not a boutique or “ultra-luxury storytelling” hotel
Can feel busy / corporate
Luxury level is solid but not on the level of ultra-premium brands nearby
🧠 Simple summary

London Hilton on Park Lane = iconic high-rise luxury hotel in Mayfair with unbeatable location, Hyde Park views, and dependable 5-star Hilton comfort.

My recent stay at London Hilton on Park Lane was, overall, a thoroughly enjoyable experience, deserving of a solid four-star commendation.

From the outset, the hotel impresses with its prime position overlooking Hyde Park, offering some of the most captivating views in London. There is an immediate sense of scale and energy upon arrival, with a lobby that is both lively and well-appointed, striking a balance between modern comfort and classic city hotel charm.

The room itself was well presented, clean, and thoughtfully arranged. While not overly extravagant, it delivered everything one would expect from a reputable establishment of this calibre. The true highlight, however, was undoubtedly the view — waking up to the London skyline added a certain refinement to the stay that elevated the overall experience.

Service throughout was professional and efficient. Staff were courteous and accommodating, particularly during check-in and within the communal areas. While there were moments where the pace felt slightly brisk — perhaps a reflection of the hotel’s popularity — it never detracted significantly from the overall comfort of the stay.

Facilities were equally commendable, with the executive spaces and dining options offering a pleasant environment to unwind. The atmosphere carries a certain cosmopolitan vibrancy, making it well suited for both business and leisure travellers.

If one were to offer a minor critique, it would be that certain aspects of the property feel a touch dated when compared to some of London’s newer luxury offerings. However, this is more than offset by its location, views, and consistently reliable service.

In summary, the London Hilton on Park Lane delivers a polished and dependable experience in one of the city’s most desirable locations. It may not seek to overwhelm with extravagance, but it succeeds in providing comfort, convenience, and a quietly confident standard of hospitality.

A very overdue review — but some stays are memorable enough that you still think about them years later.

Charlie and I stayed at the London Hilton on Park Lane in December 2022, and it was genuinely such a special experience. London in December is magical anyway, but being based in Mayfair, right by Hyde Park, just elevated the whole trip.

The hotel itself has that classic, iconic feel the moment you walk in. It’s elegant without being stuffy, luxurious without feeling intimidating. Our room was beautiful — spotless, spacious and incredibly comfortable — and the views across London were something else. Waking up to that skyline in winter felt properly cinematic.

What stood out most though was the service. The team were warm, professional and attentive throughout. Nothing felt forced or transactional — just thoughtful hospitality done really well. You can always tell when staff take pride in where they work, and that absolutely came through.

It’s the kind of place that makes you feel looked after from start to finish. Even now, thinking back to that December stay, it still feels special.

A huge thank you to everyone at the London Hilton on Park Lane for making it such a memorable visit. It’s one we won’t forget.

This was not my first stay at Hilton London Park Lane, and I decided to give the hotel a second chance, which I truly regret.

The lobby staff are extremely unhelpful, with poor attitudes and no willingness to assist. Even the managers offer no real support—everyone avoids responsibility and keeps sending you to someone else without any solution.

Housekeeping is very poor. Rooms are not cleaned unless you ask repeatedly, and even basic amenities like water are not provided unless you keep requesting them.

Due to the complete lack of comfort, I eventually had to change my booking and move to another hotel.
On top of that, the hotel changed the cancellation policy and charged additional fees, showing no flexibility or consideration.

Even the food quality has declined significantly. It used to be good in the past, but now when I return hungry and order room service, the food is very bad, tasteless, and not even properly seasoned—literally bland and unpleasant.

A very disappointing experience overall.
Does not reflect the Hilton name and is absolutely not worth the price.

My stay from 31 December to 3 January was unfortunately not a very pleasant one. I had hoped to enjoy a nice New Year’s break at the hotel.The property has a long history and, although it has been renovated, there were several comfort issues. The central air conditioning was extremely noisy, and the windows did not insulate well against the cold or wind. The room felt slightly warmer only when the curtains were fully closed. A portable heater was provided, but the room was still cold unless the noisy air conditioning was left on.There also appeared to be an air deodorising device in the room, which produced a high-pitched mechanical sound and emitted a strong disinfectant-like smell that was quite unpleasant. In addition, possibly due to ageing plumbing, there was occasionally an unpleasant odour from the bathroom if the toilet lid was left open.The room was on a low floor. Although described as facing Hyde Park, the actual view was mainly rooftops of nearby buildings. The room was positioned in a narrow gap in this buildings, meaning there was very little privacy — with the curtains open and lights on, it was easy to see into neighbouring rooms and vice versa.Unfortunately, I also left behind a new limited-edition white Hello Kitty hair band which recently purchased during another trip at check-out. I contacted the hotel within a few hours, but it could not be located despite several follow-ups. As it was white and left on the bed, it may have been overlooked during housekeeping.None of these issues were the fault of the hotel staff. The hotel was extremely busy over the New Year period, and there were no alternative rooms available.Hilton, of course, cannot be compared with five-star or ultra-luxury brands, and perhaps my expectations were too high to begin with. That said, you do get what you pay for. The pricing is relatively reasonable for the area, and the location is excellent. From a value-for-money perspective, it is still a workable option if budget and location are the main priorities.

This was my second stay at the Hilton Atlanta Airport, and unfortunately it confirmed that the shuttle and guest service issues I experienced before were not isolated incidents.

During my first stay, the airport shuttle was over an hour late. While inconvenient, the rest of the stay was fine, so I gave the hotel the benefit of the doubt and assumed it was a one-time problem.

On my most recent stay, the same issue occurred—only worse.

I arrived at the airport at 8:40 PM and was at the hotel shuttle pickup area by 8:57 PM. According to the hotel’s information and app, the shuttle runs at :15 and :45. I remained at the pickup location the entire time along with several other guests, yet no shuttle arrived at 9:15, 9:45, or 10:15 PM. The shuttle finally arrived at 10:21 PM, resulting in nearly a 1.5-hour wait.

During this time, there was no way to reach the hotel, and no updates or communication were provided.

After check-in, I asked to speak with a manager about the situation. I was told no manager was on duty and that a supervisor would address my concern. I calmly and respectfully explained the situation and timeline. The supervisor repeatedly asked how I eventually arrived at the hotel, which gave the impression that because I did arrive, the shuttle was only a few minutes late. This line of questioning completely disregarded the fact that three scheduled shuttle times were missed.

Rather than acknowledging the delay, she became defensive, implied that we somehow missed all the shuttle runs, and dismissed the situation as “no big deal.” When I continued to calmly restate the facts, she said she “couldn’t talk to me like this,” which felt disrespectful and unprofessional.

I rarely leave negative reviews and have been a loyal Hilton guest for many years, but this was one of the poorest guest service experiences I’ve had with the Hilton brand. An airport hotel should provide reliable transportation and professional, respectful customer service.

I would not recommend this property to travelers base on my encounter.

Had an amazing experience at Trader Vic’s in Atlanta, Georgia! The atmosphere was classic tiki perfection—fun, vibrant, and the perfect place to relax and enjoy top-tier cocktails. What really made the night special were the bartenders, Salam and Milenko. Both were friendly, knowledgeable, and clearly passionate about their craft.

Milenko was also fantastic, bringing great energy and service that made the whole experience even better. If you’re in Atlanta and looking for an unforgettable tiki bar experience with amazing drinks and excellent bartenders, Trader Vic’s is the place to go. Can’t wait to come back!
